biomejs/biome#1630.body:
### Environment information

```block
CLI:
  Version:                      1.5.2
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             x86_64
  OS:                           linux

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_DIR:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         "xterm-256color"
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           "v21.1.0"
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              "node"
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         "pnpm/8.10.2"

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Formatter disabled:           false
  Linter disabled:              false
  Organize imports disabled:    false
  VCS disabled:                 true

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```


### What happened?

I have several "Folders" in my VS Code "Workspace". Like this:

![image](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/assets/226640/f450d60a-35ec-47c6-9a5d-8f9e7cd67bb5)

Some of those "Folders" are projects that use Biome, and some aren't. Biome's VS Code extension doesn't handle this very gracefully. There are several issues:

### Bug 1: First "Folder" in the VS Code "Workspace" must be a Biome project for any others to work

If you order the "Folders" in your "Workspace" such that the top-most one doesn't have Biome installed, none of the files you open in the older "Folders" will run through Biome checks. It seems like the top-most "Folder" must be a Biome project for the Biome VS Code extension to do anything and will instead show this warning:

![image](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/assets/226640/ab3507af-84c6-499b-9d11-83f07fd21baf)

### Bug 2: Biome runs on "Folders" that aren't Biome projects

The inverse of Bug 1 also exists, where if the top-most "Folder" is a Biome project, all other "Folders" will get checked by Biome, even if they don't have Biome installed or configured.

### Bug 3: Duplicated problems

For every folder with Biome installed, the "Problems" tab shows duplicate Biome errors:

![image](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/assets/226640/2bb3ba0a-b1a5-4c98-af6d-f95374b77c15)

Note how each of those problems is the same problem on the same line & column. It's a duplicate.


### Expected result

Expectations listed above in each bug

### Code of Conduct

- [X] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#1630.title: 🐛 VS Code extension: Poor support for multi-folder workspaces

  Formatter disabled:           true
  Linter disabled:              false
  Organize imports disabled:    false
  VCS disabled:                 false

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```


### What happened?

In this code:
```js
function notAhook() {
	useSomeHook()
}
```

`useHookAtTopLevel` does not show an error for `useSomeHook` not being used inside a component-named or hook-named function.

[Playground link](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=all&code=aQBtAHAAbwByAHQAIAB7ACAAdQBzAGUAUwBvAG0AZQBIAG8AbwBrACAAfQAgAGYAcgBvAG0AIAAiAHMAbwBtAGUAdwBoAGUAcgBlACIACgAKAGUAeABwAG8AcgB0ACAAZgB1AG4AYwB0AGkAbwBuACAAcwBvAG0AZQBSAGUAZwB1AGwAYQByAEYAdQBuAGMAdABpAG8AbgAoACkAIAB7AAoAIAAgAHUAcwBlAFMAbwBtAGUASABvAG8AawAoACkACgB9AAoACgAKAA%3D%3D)

### Expected result

`useHookAtTopLevel` should show an error for `useSomeHook` not being used inside a component-named or hook-named function.

References:
- https://react.dev/learn/state-a-components-memory#meet-your-first-hook
- https://legacy.reactjs.org/docs/hooks-rules.html#only-call-hooks-from-react-functions

### Code of Conduct

- [X] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#1984.title: 🐛 `useHookAtTopLevel` doesn't report hooks used outside of non-hook functions

biomejs/biome#2988.body: ### Environment information ```block CLI: Version: 1.7.3 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: aarch64 OS: macos Environment: BIOME_LOG_DIR: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: "screen-256color" JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: "v20.12.2" JS_RUNTIME_NAME: "node" NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: "yarn/4.1.0" Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Formatter disabled: false Linter disabled: false Organize imports disabled: false VCS disabled: false Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ``` ### What happened? When using the stdin mode in combination with “--colors off", some characters are displayed with broken characters. I'm not a rustacean but have investigated the code. The broken characters are [Combining character](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Combining_character) which is represented by multiple code points. ```rust fn main() { println!("{}", "⚠️".escape_unicode()); // \u{26a0}\u{fe0f} println!("{}", "â".escape_unicode()); // \u{61}\u{302} println!("{}", "ｶﾞ".escape_unicode()); // \u{ff76}\u{ff9e} println!("{}", "👨🏻‍🦱".escape_unicode()); // \u{1f468}\u{1f3fb}\u{200d}\u{1f9b1} } ``` `--colors off` or not using terminal will result in `ColorChoice::Never`. https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/fec262f1593c53e4d6c46f6934e3e2ebc2144edc/crates/biome_console/src/lib.rs#L94-L114 If you run `biome format` in stdin mode, the entire code is output here. https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/fec262f1593c53e4d6c46f6934e3e2ebc2144edc/crates/biome_cli/src/execute/std_in.rs#L66-L68 Finally, this `write_str` is called. https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/fec262f1593c53e4d6c46f6934e3e2ebc2144edc/crates/biome_console/src/write/termcolor.rs#L146 In the `write_str` method, convert characters in the case of Windows or `ColorChoice::Never`. By `grapheme.chars().nth(0)`, all combining characters output only the first code point. https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/fec262f1593c53e4d6c46f6934e3e2ebc2144edc/crates/biome_console/src/write/termcolor.rs#L167-L173 ### codesandbox https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/serene-swirles-6z4jfd?embed=1&file=%2Fsrc%2Findex.js%3A5%2C1&workspaceId=6833e942-7d98-4ed2-8f40-9a265aa14215 ### related issues * https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/2604 * https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/455 ### Expected result The output of the code should not be converted anything. ### Code of Conduct - [X] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#2988.title: 🐛 biome breaks characters in stdin mode

biomejs/biome#3187.body:
### Description

This umbrella issue tracks the development of type-aware lint rules.
We first motivate our decision to implement our own type synthesizer, and then present the type-aware rules we intend to implement. In a second section, we present preliminary design material that indicates the high-level direction we want to take.

## Motivation

Multiple [rules from TypeScript ESLint](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/?=typeInformation) requires type information. Moreover, several linter rules could be enhanced by using type information.

TypeScript ESLint uses the TypeScript Compiler API to get types. This architecture has the advantage of using the TypeScript Compiler. However, it has several drawbacks:

- TSC is slow, and using the Compiler API makes the slowness even more noticeable.
  In fact, it is so slow that TypeScript ESLint provides presets with and without the rules that require type information.
- Biome and TSC use their own AST, which makes interoperability difficult.

This is why we think it is important to implement our own type synthesiser. If we have a fast type synthesiser, then we could enhance many lint rules with a marginal performance overhead. Note that we are not trying to implement a full-fledged type system or a type checker like TypeScript. Many attempt, even [the most promising](https://github.com/dudykr/stc) failed.

We believe that the Biome type synthesiser doesn't need to be perfect or handle complex TypeScript types. Even a rudimentary type synthesiser could be valuable to many lint rules.

## Type-aware lint rules

A first design and implementation of the Biome type synthesiser aims to implement a preliminary version of the following rules:

- useAwaitThenable ([await-thenable](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/await-thenable/))

  Ensure that only thenable values are awaited. We could first target a rule that ensures that an awaited expression is a Promise.  We could ignore values with an unknown type.

- noFloatingPromises ([no-floating-promises](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-floating-promises/))

  Ensure that a promise is handled (returned, awaited, ...).

- noForInArray ([no-for-in-array](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-for-in-array/))

  Ensure that `for-in` is not used on arrays.

- noDuplicateLiteralEnumMembers ([no-duplicate-enum-values](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-duplicate-enum-values))

  Ensure that every enum member initialized with a literal expression is unique. This doesn't necessarlly requires a type system. We need to compute literal expressions.

## Funding

To support this effort, please consider sponsoring Biome within our [Open Collective](https://opencollective.com/biome) or [GitHub sponsorship](https://github.com/sponsors/biomejs).

For companies wishing to speed up the development of this task, please consider hiring one of our contributors through our [Enterprise Support](https://biomejs.dev/enterprise/).
biomejs/biome#3187.title: ☂️ Type-aware linter

### Rule name

noUnusedImports

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=all&%2BACAAewAKACAAIAAgACAAZgBlAHQAYwBoAHAAcgBpAG8AcgBpAHQAeQA%2FADoAIAAiAGgAaQBnAGgAIgAgAHwAIAAiAGwAbwB3ACIAIAB8ACAAIgBhAHUAdABvACIAOwAKACAAIAB9AAoAfQAKAA%3D%3D

### Expected result

Removing the last import from a file with no exports should be an unsafe fix or should add `export {}` at the bottom.

Typescript files with no imports or exports are ambient and all their declarations become global which usually breaks something.

This can realistically happen when using interface merging, because interface merging doesn't need any export statement, which is what happened to me.

  Formatter disabled:           false
  Linter disabled:              false
  Organize imports disabled:    false
  VCS disabled:                 false

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

1. `git checkout main`
2. `echo "const a=1" > test.ts`
3. `git commit -m "1"`
4. `git checkout -b test`
5. `echo "const a=2" > test.ts`
6. `git commit -m "2"`
7. `mv test.ts test2.ts`
8. `npx biome check --changed`

```
test.ts internalError/io  INTERNAL  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ No such file or directory (os error 2)
  
  ⚠ This diagnostic was derived from an internal Biome error. Potential bug, please report it if necessary.
```


### Expected result

Should not show an error

I get that `--changed` is supposed to be used in CI, but it can also be useful locally, e.g. for precommit checks. Probably it should treat uncommited local changes the same way as commits

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#4952.title: 🐛 --changed throws No such file or directory when file is deleted

### Rule name

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=PAA%2BAAoAIAAgADwAbgBhAHYAIAByAG8AbABlAD0AIgBuAGEAdgBpAGcAYQB0AGkAbwBuACIAPgAKACAAIAAgACAAJiAKACAAIAA8AC8AbgBhAHYAPgAKACAAIAA8AGYAbwBvAHQAZQByACAAcgBvAGwAZQA9ACIAYwBvAG4AdABlAG4AdABpAG4AZgBvACIAPgAKACAAIAAgACAAJiAKACAAIAA8AC8AZgBvAG8AdABlAHIAPgAKADwALwA%2BAA%3D%3D

### Expected result

There's a false positive if role is added to a semantic element:

```
  ✖ The elements with the following roles can be changed to the following elements:
    <footer>
  
    173 │   return (
  > 174 │     <footer role="contentinfo">
```

That `role` should only have been flagged by noRedundantRoles, but NOT by useSemanticElements.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#5279.body: ### Environment information ```block CLI: Version: 1.9.4 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: aarch64 OS: macos Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: "xterm-256color" JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: "v22.13.1" JS_RUNTIME_NAME: "node" NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: "bun/1.2.4" Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Formatter disabled: false Linter disabled: false Organize imports disabled: false VCS disabled: true Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ``` ### What happened? Tabs are rendered as `→ ` (which takes up 2 spaces) and as 4 spaces for lines that are just context. In the screenshot, see that line 4 in the biome check output is showing as being vertically aligned with line 5, even though line 5 has 2 tabs while line 4 has 1 tab. This can be reproduced with a single `biome.jsonc` file and having it check itself for formatting `biome check ./biome.jsonc` ```jsonc // biome.jsonc { "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/1.9.4/schema.json", "json": { "formatter": { "trailingCommas": "all" } } } ``` ### Expected result I'd expect the context line tabs to be 2 spaces instead of 4 ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#5279.title: 🐛 Alignment of tabbed text in `biome check` output is wrong

biomejs/biome#5687.body:
### Description

Currently our GritQL plugins can report diagnostics, but they can't suggest fixes. We probably want to utilise the [rewrite operator](https://docs.grit.io/language/patterns#rewrite-operator) for this, but we'll also need more metadata for the fix, such as applicability and a description.

---

**Want to contribute?** Lets we know you are interested! We will assign you to the issue to prevent several people to work on the same issue. Don't worry, we can unassign you later if you are no longer interested in the issue! Read our [contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [analyzer contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_analyze/CONTRIBUTING.md).
biomejs/biome#5687.title: 📎 Implement fixable GritQL plugins

biomejs/biome#5885.body:
### Description

Implement a lint rule that checks whether there's a `default` export that exports the same symbol as one of the named exports. Re-exports are considered out-of-scope.

See discussion: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/discussions/5747
biomejs/biome#5885.title: 📎 Implement `noRedundantDefaultExport`

biomejs/biome#5977.body:
### Description

Currently, our `GlobalsResolver` relies on hardcoded, predefined types for globals such as `Array` and `Promise`. TypeScript however has much more extended definitions in their `.d.ts` files: https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/tree/main/src/lib

Ideally, we'd parse these `.d.ts` files at compile time or a separate codegen step and bundle them with Biome, to be used by a revised implementation of our `GlobalsResolver`.

Of course, this does mean we need improved handling of `.d.ts` files in general, but it shouldn't take much work on that front to be able to begin this task. Feel free to inquire if you're interested in this task!
biomejs/biome#5977.title: 📎 Bundle "real" global type definitions

### Rule name

lint/complexity/noUselessUndefinedInitialization

### Playground link

https://next.biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=all&files.main.%2BAAoA

### Expected result

In svelte 4, exported values in components are the component's props. Assigning a default value makes the prop optional.

In the playground, the value is not immediately exported because it needs to be renamed. This triggers the false positive for the rule.

### Rule name

lint/correctness/noUnusedImports

### Playground link

no

### Expected result

After update to v2 I got a warning that I didn't see in v1.9:

The error:
```sh
src/util/DOM/SsrDataScript.tsx:1:43 lint/correctness/noUnusedImports  FIXABLE  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ⚠ Several of these imports are unused.
  
  > 1 │ import { Build, type FunctionalComponent, h } from '@stencil/core';
      │                                           ^
    2 │ 
    3 │ const getScriptText = (dataId: number, data: unknown) => {
  
  ℹ Unused imports might be the result of an incomplete refactoring.
  
  ℹ Unsafe fix: Remove the unused imports.
  
    1 │ import·{·Build,·type·FunctionalComponent,·h·}·from·'@stencil/core';
      │                                           --     
```

With my tsconfig:
```json
{  
  "compilerOptions": {
    "jsx": "react",  
    "jsxFactory": "h"
  }
}
```

and I tried 2 options for JSX factory, got the same warning.

biome.json
```json
{  
  "$schema": "./node_modules/@biomejs/biome/configuration_schema.json",  
  "assist": {  
    "actions": {  
      "source": {  
        "organizeImports": "on"  
      }  
    }  
  },
  "javascript": {  
    "jsxRuntime": "transparent/reactClassic",  // tried both options, with the same result
    "formatter": {  
      "quoteStyle": "single"  
    }  
  }
}
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Expected result

Any declarations are allowed inside the @namespace rules in @font-feature-values. It's how you define custom idents. The `noUnknownProperty` rule should not trigger.

See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@font-feature-values#using_styleset_in_a_font-feature-values_rule

### Rule name

noImportCycles

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noImportCycles&%3D%3D

### Expected result

The follow type of code triggers noImportCycles on biome cli, but not in the editor or playground.
```ts
export function example1() {
  return 1;
}

export function example2() {
  return 2;
}

export * as Example from './test';
```

Example:
```
packages/core/src/event-bus/client.ts:181:27 lint/nursery/noImportCycles ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ This import is part of a cycle.

    179 │ }
    180 │
  > 181 │ export * as EventBus from './client';
        │                           ^^^^^^^^^^
    182 │

  ℹ This import resolves to packages/core/src/event-bus/client.ts
        ... which imports packages/core/src/event-bus/client.ts
        ... which is the file we're importing from.
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#6657.body:
### Description

Addresses https://github.com/biomejs/biome/discussions/1726

This issue serves as an entry point to collect tasks, comments, and feedback regarding the support of Vue, Svelte and Astro files inside Biome.

We don't have a definitive solution, architecturally speaking, for it yet, as highlighted in https://github.com/biomejs/biome/discussions/1726

### Parser

The support for those languages requires the next tasks:
- Expand a few aspects of the parsers to support `.astro` files
  - support the `---` / `---` frontmatter
  - support JSX-like syntax
- Expand possible aspects to support Vue and Svelte files
- Enhance HTML parsing to define possible templating nodes e.g. `{{ variable }}`
- Creates syntax nodes that can be sent across threads and that aren't necessarily roots

### Semantic models

We will need to create new semantic models. It's not clear if these semantic models need to be per language, or if we can have one single, generic, model for all templating languages. 

What we want to achieve is the ability to collect bindings from the JavaScript code of the template, so we can analyse them when analyze the HTML part of the template e.g. 

```vue
<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue'

const count = ref(0) // `const` is automatically exported
</script>

<template>
   <!-- Here we should be able to query `count` and make sure it exists -->
  <button @click="count++">You clicked me {{ count }} times.</button>
</template>
```

### Formatter

If we're able to implement #3334, I believe we can use the same principle
biomejs/biome#6657.title: ☂️ HTML-ish languages

  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

```grit
or {
  `import $stuff from $source`,
  `import * as $stuff from $source`
} where {
    $source <: includes "next/",
    $filename <: includes "src",
    register_diagnostic(
        span = $source,
        message = "Don't use next stuff in logic",
        severity = "error"
    )
  }
```

`$filename` never matches. If I remove or negate the `$filename` clause then the rule matches fine.

### Expected result

`$filename` should be set and match based on the path of the file that contains the node, like it is when writing normal gritql patterns using the grit cli directly. Most of the rules I want to write are for internal devops, and based on areas of code like this, based on the path.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#6783.body:
### Environment information

```block
Version:                      2.0.6
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             x86_64
  OS:                           linux

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         xterm-kitty
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v24.3.0
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         pnpm/10.12.4

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  false

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

1. create a biome.json with the following contents:
```json
{
	"$schema": "./node_modules/@biomejs/biome/configuration_schema.json",
	"files": {
		"includes": [
			"apps/**/*.{json,md,toml,ts,tsx,yaml,yml}",
			"packages/**/*.{json,md,toml,ts,tsx,yaml,yml}"
		]
	},
	"formatter": {
		"enabled": true
	}
}
```

2. run `echo '' | biome format --stdin-file-path 'a.tsx' --files-ignore-unknown=false`
3. Observe
```
The content was not formatted because the formatter is currently disabled.
stdin ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ The contents aren't fixed. Use the `--write` flag to fix them.
```



### Expected result

The fact that `a.tsx` isn't in my includes shouldn't cause format to stop working.

you have to put whatever file you have in `stdin-file-path` in your includes for it to work:

```json
{
	"$schema": "./node_modules/@biomejs/biome/configuration_schema.json",
	"files": {
		"includes": [
			"apps/**/*.{json,md,toml,ts,tsx,yaml,yml}",
			"packages/**/*.{json,md,toml,ts,tsx,yaml,yml}",
			"a.tsx"
		]
	},
	"formatter": {
		"enabled": true
	}
}
```

however this is impractical for command line tools that integrate with biome outside of the editor directly

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#6783.title: bug: `biome format` with `--stdin-file-path` fails if `includes` is missing the value in `--stdin-file-path`.

  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true

  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

I have a pnpm/biome workspace located at `myrepo/subdir/myworkspace/`.
I have this config:

```json
  "vcs": {
    "enabled": true,
    "clientKind": "git",
    "useIgnoreFile": true,
    "root": "../.."
  }
```

Whatever I put as `root`, I get `Biome couldn't find an ignore file in the following folder: /home/username/Documents/stuff/myrepo/subdir/myworkspace`

EDIT: __this issue does not appear in my similar project, it seems to be triggered by the fact that I'm in a workspace.__

### Expected result

Biome finds the ignore file.
This issue doesn't happen on v2.1.1, appeared on v2.1.2

### Workaround

Just add an empty `.gitignore` in the workspace dir (where the biome config lives).

### Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#6970.body:
### Description

Concise reporter is a way to report the diagnostics using less vertical space (and providing fewer details). 

This reporter can be enabled by requesting `--reporter=concise` in CLI args.

It works as follows (stderr and stdout use matches how they are now used by pretty formatter):

* All formatter diagnostics ("formatter would have printed the following content") are aggregated and reported to stderr in a trailing section titled "The following file(s) would be reformatted:" followed by a newline-separated indented list of violating files. 
* All other diagnostics are reported together to stderr, one line per diagnostic, containing:
  - severity icon
  - file path and lineno
  - full error code
  - short summary (one used as a "header" in current verbose formatter)
* The output ends with a summary written to stdout, reporting amount of checked files, warnings, errors, and files to be reformatted. 

Here's an example (colors should be similar to colors used by pretty reporter):

```
$ pbiome check a.ts b.ts
⚠ a.ts:1: lint/style/useImportType: Some named imports are only used as types.
⚠ a.ts:1: lint/correctness/noUnusedImports: These imports are unused.
✖ a.ts:3: lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables: This type alias Q is unused.
✖ a.ts:13: lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables: This function func1 is unused.
✖ a.ts:15: lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables: This function func2 is unused.
✖ b.ts:10: lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables: This function something is unused.
✖ b.ts:11: lint/correctness/noConstantCondition: Unexpected constant condition.

The following files would be reformatted:
    a.ts
    b.ts

Checked 2 files in 227ms. No fixes applied.
Found 5 errors, 2 warnings, 2 files that would be reformatted.
```

Yes, this means some rule summaries might be somewhat unclear. That can be improved  gradually and should not be a blocker for making concise formatter available.

Discussed&approved in #6948 and previously requested in #1909, see those for motivation and context.
biomejs/biome#6970.title: 📎 Support `--reporter=concise` for less verbose diagnostics

### Rule name

noUnknownPseudoClass

### Playground link

https://github.com/jagregory/biome-repro-1753226182692

### Expected result

tl;dr CSS Modules `:local` pseudo-selector used to work pre-2.1, I don't know exactly when it changed but I just upgraded from 1.9.5 to 2.1 and it's now being flagged as an error.

```
  ✖ Unexpected unknown pseudo-class local

  > 1 │ .ui:global div:local(.example) {
      │                ^^^^^
    2 │ 	padding: 0;
    3 │ }
```

CSS Modules support a `:global` psuedo-class to refer to a class outside of CSS Modules, this works fine in Biome; however, CSS Modules also supports a `:local` pseudo-class for use when you've already "escaped" into the global scope, and want to refer back to a local class. This `local` pseudo-class does not work since sometime between 1.9.5 and 2.1.

For example. I have `.ui` defined externally, outside of CSS Modules, which will be present in the final CSS as `.ui`. I also have a CSS Module for a specific component with an `.example` class, this will be hashed and end up in the final CSS with some generated name like `.example-HASH`.

The above CSS snippet lets you write a selector like `.ui div.example-HASH`.

This is fairly uncommon in my experience, but sometimes needed when you're mixing CSS Modules with unbundled external CSS.

Refs:
- https://github.com/css-modules/css-modules/issues/264
- https://github.com/webpack-contrib/css-loader#scope

### Code of Conduct

### Expected result

I would expect that the casing of the function name (that returns a React component) is irrelevant.

It seems that whenever the first character is uppercase, the rule triggers a false positive. In the playground, you can see that `getComponent` does not raise any errors.

### Rule name

`nursery/noShadow`

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lineWidth=120&indentStyle=space&lintRules=noShadow&%3D&language=ts

### Expected result

Biome should _probably_ not throw any errors within the constructor implementation, and certainly none within subsequent methods.

The overloaded parameters are nonexistent at runtime and serve as syntactic sugar, so little confusion should arise from something like 
```ts
constructor(p1: string);
constructor(p1: number);
constructor(p1: unknown) {};
```

As for the errors on subsequent methods, using the same variable name in a subsequent method is still a wholly separate scope and thus not likely to be conflated.
```ts
class counter {
  private count: number;
  constructor (count: number);
  constructor (count: BigInt);
  constructor (count: any) {this.count = count};

  increment(count: number) {
    this.count += count
  }
}
```

This issue appears unique to Biome since [typescript-eslint](https://typescript-eslint.io/play/#ts=5.9.2&fileType=.tsx&code=MYGwhgzhAEBmD29oG8CwAoa0AOAne2AXNAHYCuAtgEYCmu0APtFYiDWCQNwYZbDwkIAF1xlgQ%2BLgAU2MLjAUAjMXLU6ASm6Zo-QSLETp8IQAs6AOQU1iLeGw6beOgcNHjJMuQuXNW7EoyklLS46ihOWKYAlhAAdHgE0AC8OF5KWlh8LkKp8krJ0ACsnNAA9KXQAKK4%2BLgQADTQkNA0AB7YNOI0ACaxEc560MZmuJYUNAUALCXl0AAi8DQQJADkOXS1jc1tHV29TgC%2BPNqyeYoFxcdYs%2BZIG5IwIxM0UaZ0AIRO46bw3Z5nxFcURIAHNHNpviZflJhhYrDY-A4MtBIdDTt5ASJgSDArZ7CQwshoP02DlYaMrFNkUd0DSMKBIDB%2BGQSEI6OETrgogA3MBs5wsoQqYJ0ZG6VwGDzM1nCtShMXZNyGKTSoXQABCURBAElWeCsnolVL4ILiBwAJ6E6DROKqgp2g5aJzA4C4GjjVkqk0yoJywn9G2xO0AahSqsOGAOQA&eslintrc=N4KABGBEBOCuA2BTAzpAXGUEKQHYHsBaZACwEMATfAd3SkWmn2knDAF8R2g&tsconfig=N4KABGBEDGD2C2AHAlgGwKYCcDyiAuysAdgM6QBcYoEEkJemy0eAcgK6qoDCAFutAGsylBm3TgwAXxCSgA&tokens=false) handles the exact same kind of overloaded constructors A-OK.

To clarify, something like this should probably still error:
```ts
class foo {
  constructor(a: string);
  constructor(a: number);
  constructor(a: unknown) {
    // shadows `a` in overload implementation parameter
    let a = 4;
  }
}
```

, merely that THIS shouldn't:
```ts
class foo {
  constructor(a: string);
  constructor(a: number);
  constructor(a: unknown) {}
}
```

I hope i didn't do too poorly with the request. this is my first time so a little nervous 

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#7125.title: 💅 `nursery/noShadow` incorrectly flags Typescript constructor overload parameters in constructor implementation, class methods

biomejs/biome#7138.body:
### Environment information (from `./frontend`)

```block
CLI:
  Version:                      2.1.3
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             x86_64
  OS:                           linux

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         xterm-256color
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v22.17.1
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         npm/10.9.2

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

I'm experiencing an issue where the Biome VS Code extension seems to ignore the  configuration in my `biome.json` file when it's located in a subdirectory of a devcontainer workspace. **Specifically, this happens during `formatOnSave` operations.** For Example, The formatter uses tabs (`\t`) instead of spaces despite the explicit space configuration. Could you help me determine if this is a bug or a configuration issue on my end?

## Project Structure

```text
/workspaces/project/
├── .vscode/
│   └── settings.json          # VS Code workspace settings
├── .devcontainer/
│   └── devcontainer.json     # devcontainer configuration
├── frontend/                  # Sub-directory containing Biome config
│   ├── biome.json            # Biome configuration file
│   ├── package.json
│   └── node_modules/
│       └── @biomejs/cli-linux-x64/
│           └── biome         # Biome binary
└── ...
```

## Configuration Files

### VS Code Settings (`.vscode/settings.json`)

```json
{
  "editor.tabSize": 2,
  "editor.formatOnSave": true,
  "editor.defaultFormatter": "biomejs.biome",
  "editor.codeActionsOnSave": {
    "source.fixAll.biome": "explicit",
    "source.organizeImports.biome": "never"
  },
  "[javascript]": {
    "editor.defaultFormatter": "biomejs.biome"
  },
  "[typescript]": {
    "editor.defaultFormatter": "biomejs.biome"
  },
  "[javascriptreact]": {
    "editor.defaultFormatter": "biomejs.biome"
  },
  "[typescriptreact]": {
    "editor.defaultFormatter": "biomejs.biome"
  },
  "[json]": {
    "editor.defaultFormatter": "biomejs.biome"
  },
  "biome.lsp.bin": "./frontend/node_modules/@biomejs/cli-linux-x64/biome",
  "biome.configurationPath": "./frontend/biome.json"
}
```

### Biome Configuration (`frontend/biome.json`)

```json
{
  "$schema": "./node_modules/@biomejs/biome/configuration_schema.json",
  "vcs": {
    "enabled": true,
    "clientKind": "git",
    "useIgnoreFile": true
  },
  "files": {
    "ignoreUnknown": false,
    "includes": ["**/*", "!dist/**", "!node_modules/**", "!**/*.d.ts"]
  },
  "formatter": {
    "enabled": true,
    "indentStyle": "space",
    "indentWidth": 2,
    "lineWidth": 120,
    "lineEnding": "lf",
    "formatWithErrors": false
  },
  "javascript": {
    "formatter": {
      "enabled": true,
      "quoteStyle": "single",
      "jsxQuoteStyle": "single",
      "semicolons": "always",
      "trailingCommas": "es5",
      "arrowParentheses": "asNeeded",
      "bracketSpacing": true,
      "bracketSameLine": false,
      "quoteProperties": "asNeeded"
    }
  },
  "json": {
    "formatter": {
      "enabled": true,
      "indentStyle": "space"
    }
  }
}
```

### VS Code Output Log

```text
2025-08-06 18:47:05.390 [info] [Trace - 6:47:05 PM] Received response 'textDocument/formatting - (7)' in 2ms.
2025-08-06 18:47:05.390 [info] Result: [
    {
        "newText": "\t",
        "range": {
            "end": {
                "character": 7,
                "line": 50
            },
            "start": {
                "character": 5,
                "line": 50
            }
        }
    }
]
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code in devcontainer environment
2. Navigate to a TypeScript/JavaScript file in the `frontend/` directory
3. Add some code with inconsistent indentation (using tabs)
4. **Save the file (this triggers `formatOnSave` since `"editor.formatOnSave": true` is enabled)**
5. Observe that the file is formatted with tabs instead of spaces

## Workaround Attempts

None found yet. The issue persists despite:

- Correct binary path configuration (`biome.lsp.bin`)
- Correct configuration path (`biome.configurationPath`)
- Valid JSON configuration file
- Working Biome CLI (can format correctly when run manually)

### Expected result

The formatter should replace indentation with 2 spaces, not tabs.

Expected log output should be:

```json
{
    "newText": "  ",  // 2 spaces instead of \t
    "range": {
        "end": {
            "character": 7,
            "line": 50
        },
        "start": {
            "character": 5,
            "line": 50
        }
    }
}
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#7138.title: 🐛 Biome VS Code Extension Configuration Issue when format on save

biomejs/biome#7296.body:
### Environment information

```block
Biome + VSCode extension
```

### What happened?

Biome prevents files/folders from being deleted or even to open sometimes.
This is a breaking bug that makes biome literally unusable.
1. Install VSCode on windows machine
2. Install biome and extension, start it, make sure "Biome is ready."
3. Open js project, try to manipulate files via even with something like npm ci - you will fail.

### Expected result

I expect it to not interfere with file system.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Environment information

```block

Platform:

  OS:                           macos

Environment:

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         /Users/chrisgrieser/.config/biome.jsonc
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  false

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

I have a monorepo, with two biome configs:

```
~/.config/biome.jsonc
~/.config/obsidian/vimrc/biome.jsonc
```

The second one has the following content:

```json
{
	"root": false,
	"extends": ["../../biome.jsonc"],
}
```

However, the child config is ignored, and running `biome check .` in the `~/.config/obsidian/vimrc` results in this error, even though the biome.jsonc in the repo root exists and is a valid biome configuration working elsewhere in the monorepo (where there is no child config)

```
> biome check .
configuration ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ Failed to resolve the configuration from ../../biome.jsonc

    Caused by:
      Could not resolve ../../biome.jsonc: module not found


configuration ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ Biome exited because the configuration resulted in errors. Please fix them.
```

This is pretty much the issue described in #6483, which was said to be fixed in biome 2.1, but still exists for me.

repro-repo: https://github.com/chrisgrieser/biome-repro-1756309977877

### Expected result

child configuration should work.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#7343.title: 🐛 `extends` is not able to resolve configuration file in monorepo when child config is two levels deep

biomejs/biome#7345.body:
### Description

Implement vue setup script lint rule which checks that specified macros are defined first.

```ts
const message = ref()
defineProps() // defineProps should go first
```

This won't be restricted to builtin macros, because external libraries like unplugin-vue-router also provider their own.

https://eslint.vuejs.org/rules/define-macros-order

### What happened?

1. Install deps with pnpm
1. Run pnpm check - it should work without issues
1. Run pnpm check:strict - it should throw an issue with noConsole


Discord ticket link: https://discord.com/channels/1132231889290285117/1412802027964203119

### Expected result

One command should fail, the other should exit without errors. But it looks like that even when running biome.strict.json it gets overridden by biome.json, which should not happen

The context is that i want to have strict config for CI usage (and cli in general) and more relaxed one for IDE - so it does not yell at me while im still writing the code. The relaxed one should be called just biome.json so it gets picked up by various IDEs without additional configuration.

biomejs/biome#7499.body:
### Description

currently no_unused_private_class_members is based on an eslint rule, whereas use_readonly_class_properties is used on typescript/eslint rule.

1. Why consistency matters
	•	Developer experience: Users of Biome will expect similar behavior across rules. For example, how unused private members are detected should align with how readonly properties are suggested.
	•	Predictable diagnostics: If both rules analyze class members differently, it can lead to conflicting suggestions — e.g., one says “this private member is unused” while another says “make it readonly.”
	•	Easier maintenance: Shared logic for things like detecting accessors, TS vs ES private members, and reference tracking reduces duplication in your codebase.

⸻

2. What to align
	1.	Member detection
	•	Both rules should normalize all class members (properties, accessors, TS property parameters, etc.) into a consistent internal representation (AnyMember or similar).
	•	This makes it easy to apply the same traversal logic (all_references, reads/writes) across rules.
	2.	Handling accessors
	•	Decide on one strategy for getter/setter pairs: either treat them as a single logical member or separate members but mark them specially.
	•	Both rules should behave the same way when accessors exist.
	3.	Private vs public
	•	Keep the distinction between ES #private and TS private consistent in both rules.
	•	This helps rules like prefer-readonly to ignore computed properties if no-unused-private-class-members also ignores them.
	4.	Reference tracking
	•	Centralize reads vs writes detection in a utility module.
	•	Both rules can then use the same ClassMemberReferences structure.
	5.	Diagnostics & auto-fixes
	•	Make messages and fix strategies consistent:
	•	Example: unused private member → remove or rename with _.
	•	Example: readonly suggestion → same style of JsRuleAction.
biomejs/biome#7499.title: Align no_unused_private_class_members and use_readonly_class_properties behaviour

  Path:                         biome.jsonc
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

A `lint/correctness/noUnusedImports` error is always triggered when I define a variable with the same name as an imported type namespace object.

#### Program that triggers the error

```ts
// lint/correctness/noUnusedImports: This import is unused.
import type * as hast from "hast"

const _ = () => {
  let hast: hast.Root
}
```

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=aQBtAHAAbwByAHQAIAB0AHkAcABlACAAKgAgAGEAcwAgAGgAYQBzAHQAIABmAHIAbwBtACAAIgBoAGEAcwB0ACIACgAKAGMAbwBuAHMAdAAgAF8AIAA9ACAAKAApACAAPQA%2BACAAewAKACAAIABsAGUAdAAgAGgAYQBzAHQAOgAgAGgAYQBzAHQALgBSAG8AbwB0AAoAfQA%3D

#### Program that does not trigger the error

This just changes the variable name to `hoge` from `hast`.

```ts
import type * as hast from "hast"

const _ = () => {
  let hoge: hast.Root 
}
```

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=aQBtAHAAbwByAHQAIAB0AHkAcABlACAAKgAgAGEAcwAgAGgAYQBzAHQAIABmAHIAbwBtACAAIgBoAGEAcwB0ACIACgAKAGMAbwBuAHMAdAAgAF8AIAA9ACAAKAApACAAPQA%2BACAAewAKACAAIABsAGUAdAAgAGgAbwBnAGUAOgAgAGgAYQBzAHQALgBSAG8AbwB0AAoAfQA%3D

### Expected result

`lint/correctness/noUnusedImports` error should not be triggered.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Rule name

lint/complexity/useOptionalChain

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=useOptionalChain&analyzerFixMode=safeAndUnsafeFixes&code=YwBvAG4AcwBvAGwAZQAuAGwAbwBnACgAdAB5AHAAZQBvAGYAIAB3AGkAbgBkAG8AdwAgACEAPQA9ACAAIgB1AG4AZABlAGYAaQBuAGUAZAAiACAAJgAmACAAdwBpAG4AZABvAHcALgBsAG8AYwBhAHQAaQBvAG4AKQA7AAoA&language=ts

### Expected result

The following snippets of code are not equivalent:
```ts
typeof window !== "undefined" && window.location
```
```ts
window?.location
```
They are obviously inequivalent in the sense that the first can give `false` while the second can give `undefined`. This does not matter in practice, so I'm happy to apply the unsafe fix for the rule for local variables.

The issue is with global variables. The above snippets are essentially equivalent when run in a browser. But when run on a server (code like the above is very common in server-side React), the first one will return `false` while the second will throw a `ReferenceError`.

Suggestion: allow configuring a list of variable names (e.g. `["document", "window"]`) not to apply the fix to

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Rule name

noUselessEscapeInString

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noUselessEscapeInString&

### Expected result

`\${var} `and `$\{var}` both disable substitutions but Biome strips the \ for the second one

```bash
❯ cat test.ts 
const b = "b";

console.log(`a${b}c`);
console.log(`a\${b}c`);  
console.log(`a$\{b}c`);   

❯ tsx test.ts 
abc
a${b}c
a${b}c

❯ biome lint --write test.ts 
Checked 1 file in 7ms. Fixed 1 file.

❯ cat test.ts 
const b = "b";

console.log(`a${b}c`);
console.log(`a\${b}c`);  
console.log(`a${b}c`);   

❯ tsx test.ts 
abc
a${b}c
abc
```

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Rule name

the linter in general

### Playground link

see below for repro command

### Expected result

To reproduce, here's a bash command that creates a temporary directory, a javascript file with a lint error, and demonstrates that both `biome lint --error-on-warnings` and `biome check --error-on-warnings` lint returns a zero status code:

```bash
TMP=$(mktemp -d) && cd $TMP && \
  npm init -y && \
  echo '{ "files": { "includes": ["**/*.{mjs,js,mts,ts}"] } }' > biome.jsonc && \
  echo 'try {\n	console.log("hi");\n} catch (e) {\n	throw e;\n}' > test.js && \
  npm install --save-dev --save-exact @biomejs/biome && \
  npx biome check --error-on-warnings && \
  echo "exit code: $?" && \
  npx biome lint --error-on-warnings && \
  echo "exit code: $?"
```

The [`corner-shape`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/corner-shape) property, along with its variations in names and values, is currently supported by Chromium-based browsers. However, as a relatively new rule set, it’s not yet included in the `lint/correctness/noUnknownProperty` rule.

### Environment information
```bash
/main.css:2:3 [lint/correctness/noUnknownProperty](https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-unknown-property) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  > 2 │   corner-shape: squircle;
      │   ^^^^^^^^^^^^
    3 │   corner-top-left-shape: notch;
    4 │   corner-start-start-shape: superellipse(3);
  
  ℹ See [CSS Specifications and browser specific properties](https://stylelint.io/user-guide/rules/property-no-unknown/) for more details.
  
  ℹ To resolve this issue, replace the unknown property with a valid CSS property.
```

biomejs/biome#7631.body:
### Description

[React 19.2](https://react.dev/blog/2025/10/01/react-19-2) adds two built-ins that Biome should understand:

### Summary
React 19.2 introduces two new built-ins that Biome should support:  
- [The new built-in hook](https://react.dev/blog/2025/10/01/react-19-2#use-effect-event) `useEffectEvent` has a stable, ref-like return value that **must not** be added to dependency arrays (should be treated like [`useRef`](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/fa4fcd355f8bce46b83a60fc2a874bfa93c2ce4f/crates/biome_js_analyze/src/lint/correctness/use_exhaustive_dependencies.rs#L293-L306)).  
- [The new built-in component](https://react.dev/blog/2025/10/01/react-19-2#activity) `<Activity />` should be added to the list of [known React API elements](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/fa4fcd355f8bce46b83a60fc2a874bfa93c2ce4f/crates/biome_js_analyze/src/react.rs#L140).

### Why
- Align Biome’s `useExhaustiveDependencies` rule with React 19.2 semantics so developers don’t get false positives when using Effect Events.  
- Keep Biome’s React API list up to date by recognising new built-ins introduced in React releases.

### Proposed changes
- In [`use_exhaustive_dependencies.rs`](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_js_analyze/src/lint/correctness/use_exhaustive_dependencies.rs#L293-L306), treat `useEffectEvent` like `useRef`:  
  - Calls to `useEffectEvent` should be recognised as producing a stable, ref-like value that is **never** added to dependency arrays.  
  - Example:  
    ```tsx
    const onConnected = useEffectEvent(() => { ... });
    useEffect(() => { ... }, []); // should not warn about missing `onConnected`
    ```
  - Update the test suite by adding representative cases to [valid.js](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_js_analyze/tests/specs/correctness/useExhaustiveDependencies/valid.js) (no missing dep warning for Effect Events) and [missingDependenciesInvalid.jsx](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_js_analyze/tests/specs/correctness/useExhaustiveDependencies/missingDependenciesInvalid.jsx#L11) (ensure adding an Effect Event is flagged as unnecessary).

- In [`react.rs`](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_js_analyze/src/react.rs#L140), add:  
  - `useEffectEvent` to the known React Hooks list.  
  - `Activity` to the known React Components list.  
  - Example:  
    ```tsx
    import { Activity } from "react";
    <Activity />; // should be recognised as a React API element
    ```
biomejs/biome#7631.title: 📎 React 19.2: add useEffectEvent semantics to useExhaustiveDependencies and recognise <Activity />

### Rule name

lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noUnusedVariables&code=bgBhAG0AZQBzAHAAYQBjAGUAIABNAHkAQwBvAG0AcABvAG4AZQBuAHQAIAB7AAoAIAAgACAAIABlAHgAcABvAHIAdAAgAGkAbgB0AGUAcgBmAGEAYwBlACAAUAByAG8AcABzACAAZQB4AHQAZQBuAGQAcwAgAEMAbwBtAHAAbwBuAGUAbgB0AFAAcgBvAHAAcwA8ACcAZABpAHYAJwA%2BACAAewB9AAoAfQAKAAoAZQB4AHAAbwByAHQAIABmAHUAbgBjAHQAaQBvAG4AIABNAHkAQwBvAG0AcABvAG4AZQBuAHQAKABwAHIAbwBwAHMAOgAgAE0AeQBDAG8AbQBwAG8AbgBlAG4AdAAuAFAAcgBvAHAAcwApACAAewAKACAAIAAgACAAcgBlAHQAdQByAG4AIAA8AGQAaQB2ACAAewAuAC4ALgBwAHIAbwBwAHMAfQAgAC8APgAKAH0A

### Expected result

A pattern gaining popularity recently is to define the props of a component in the namespace, which will allow access to those props to other files importing that component. However, biome is flagging that namespace as unused. I feel like this is in error, and if it's not, it would be nice to have a way to configure to ignore for namespaces or something similar. I don't want to disable this rule as it's a very critical rule for our team, but we also try avoiding littering our codebase with comments to ignore lines. Right now, there are a TON of warnings happening on our project due to this.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#7703.body:
## Description

The codegen task (likely `just new-js-lintrule`) incorrectly appends duplicate `pub mod` declarations to `crates/biome_rule_options/src/lib.rs` when creating a new rule with a name that already exists in another language category.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a rule in one language category (e.g., CSS) named `no_unknown_property`
2. Try to create a rule in another language category (e.g., JavaScript) with the same name using `just new-js-lintrule noUnknownProperty`
3. Observe that the codegen appends a duplicate entry to `biome_rule_options/src/lib.rs`

## Expected Behavior

The codegen should check if a `pub mod` entry already exists before appending to the file.

## Actual Behavior

Duplicate entries are created, causing a compilation error:

```
error[E0428]: the name `no_unknown_property` is defined multiple times
   --> crates/biome_rule_options/src/lib.rs:370:1
    |
191 | pub mod no_unknown_property;
    | ---------------------------- previous definition of the module `no_unknown_property` here
...
370 | pub mod no_unknown_property;
    | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ `no_unknown_property` redefined here
```

## Workaround

Manually edit `crates/biome_rule_options/src/lib.rs` to remove the duplicate entry.

## Related

- Issue: #7657
- Comment: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/7657#issuecomment-2559078668
- Reported by: @dibashthapa
- Requested by: @dyc3
biomejs/biome#7703.title: Codegen task appends duplicate entries to biome_rule_options/src/lib.rs for cross-language rules

biomejs/biome#7706.body:
### Description

At the moment, [`formatWithErrors`](https://biomejs.dev/reference/configuration/#formatterformatwitherrors) is exposed via configuration only. We should expose it via CLI too.

It should be really easy to implement:
- update the `#[bpaf]` derive
- handle the merging for the final config (`check` and `format` commands)
- add a test
biomejs/biome#7706.title: 📎 Expose `formatWithErrors` via CLI

### Rule name

https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-import-extensions/#forcejsextensions

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?files.main.svelte=PABzAGMAcgBpAHAAdAA%2BAAoAaQBtAHAAbwByAHQAIABGAG8AbwAgAGYAcgBvAG0AIAAiAC4ALwBmAG8AbwAuAHMAdgBlAGwAdABlACIAOwAKADwALwBzAGMAcgBpAHAAdAA%2BAA%3D%3D&files.foo.svelte=PABkAGkAdgA%2BAGgAaQA8AC8AZABpAHYAPgA%3D&files.biome.

### Expected result

I was using this rule to enforce importing `.ts` files as `.js` files. The reason I was doing this was because in my monorepo setup, the way we build packages causes `.ts` imports to not work for consumers of that package (feels like something my bundler should handle, but it doesn't).

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#7734.title: 💅 `useImportExtensions` with `forcejsextensions: true` invalidates `.css`, `.vue`, `.svelte` imports

biomejs/biome#7796.body:
### Description

There are a total of ~50 rules the last time I checked (see their list [here](https://github.com/mskelton/eslint-plugin-playwright/tree/main?tab=readme-ov-file#rules)). I am happy to port the rest of them over in smaller chunks, by the way... but this is my first Rust project contribution, so it _may_ take me a bit of time.

### Rules

| Rule | Description |
|--------|--------|
| `missingPlaywrightAwait` - [Rule](https://github.com/mskelton/eslint-plugin-playwright/blob/main/src/rules/missing-playwright-await.ts) - [Test](https://github.com/mskelton/eslint-plugin-playwright/blob/main/src/rules/missing-playwright-await.test.ts) | Identify false positives when async Playwright APIs are not properly awaited. |
| `noPlaywrightElementHandle` - [Rule](https://github.com/mskelton/eslint-plugin-playwright/blob/main/src/rules/no-element-handle.ts) - [Test](https://github.com/mskelton/eslint-plugin-playwright/blob/main/src/rules/no-element-handle.test.ts) | The use of ElementHandle is discouraged, use Locator instead. |

### Environment information ```block CLI: Version: 2.3.0 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: aarch64 OS: macos Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset BIOME_THREADS: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: xterm-256color JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: v20.18.1 JS_RUNTIME_NAME: node NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: bun/1.2.20 Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Path: biome.jsonc Formatter enabled: true Linter enabled: true Assist enabled: true VCS enabled: true Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ``` ### What happened? while open an astro file, biome crashed, i get this error in `Biome LSP` output ``` 2025-10-24 18:06:08.046 [info] Biome encountered an unexpected error This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue. When opening the issue, please provide a minimal reproduction, or identify and share the file/code that triggers it. Without a way to reproduce the error, the error can't be fixed: Source Location: /Users/runner/work/biome/biome/crates/biome_parser/src/lib.rs:888:13 Thread Name: tokio-runtime-worker Message: could not downcast root node to language biome_html_syntax::syntax_node::HtmlLanguage ``` there is my code and config ```astro --- import { Counter } from '@/components/counter' import PageLayout from '@/components/page-layout/index.astro' // Welcome to Astro! Wondering what to do next? Check out the Astro documentation at https://docs.astro.build // Don't want to use any of this? Delete everything in this file, the `assets`, `components`, and `layouts` directories, and start fresh. ---
------
``` ```jsonc // biome.jsonc { "html": { "experimentalFullSupportEnabled": true } } ``` ### Expected result not crashed and format my astro code ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### What happened?

In #7835 I discovered that `tailwindDirectives` does not work with `pnpm biome ci`, however a separate issue seems to be that the `biome ci` command doesn't respect `--css-linter-enabled=false --css-formatter-enabled=false` so its not possible to turn off CSS linting when using the `biome ci` command.

Example:

```bash
$ pnpm biome ci --css-linter-enabled=false --css-formatter-enabled=false

### Environment information

  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?cssModules=true&code=QABpAG0AcABvAHIAdAAgACIAdABhAGkAbAB3AGkAbgBkAGMAcwBzACIAOwAKAAoAQAB0AGgAZQBtAGUAIAB7AAoAIAAgAC0ALQAqADoAIABpAG4AaQB0AGkAYQBsADsACgB9AAoA&language=css

When a CSS file contains the Tailwind CSS @theme at-rule along with its internal CSS variable declaration (--*: initial;), the Biome CSS Linter fails to parse the file correctly, resulting in a series of parsing and linting errors.

This is commonly used when adopting Tailwind CSS v4 or using the PostCSS Nesting plugin with Tailwind.

### Expected result

The Biome CSS Parser and Linter should correctly parse the file without errors. The declaration --*: initial; is an intentional part of the Tailwind CSS configuration for managing CSS layers, and its usage is documented by Tailwind [here](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/theme#using-a-custom-theme).

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

Ignoring paths from Tailwind’s static analysis causes a parser error:

```css
@source not "../routes/utils/ds.tsx";

No parser error.

biomejs/biome#7849.body:
### Description

Skipping the `project` domain can speed up agentic workflows and local development when linting + formatting is desired since the `project` domain is very heavy and rarely has errors. This allows devs+agents to run the full check that CI would do very quickly for iteration rather than having to wait sometimes 10x as long just for the project rules.

[Relevant conversation thread in Discord](https://discord.com/channels/1132231889290285117/1431301990838964224)

biomejs/biome#7855.body: ### Documentation URL https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-banned-types/ ### Description 1. `Number` is not an alias of `number` according to TS - it's a reference to the boxed primitive `Number` object which is surprisingly non number-like. Saying they're used as a conventional synonym is just *wrong* (represent wholly different things) 2. The "don't use `{}`" part of the docs probably should mention `object` as an "arbitrary object" type as is done in [`tseslint`](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-empty-object-type) - This extends to the error message as well, albeit slightly less bad (we do mention Record though as an analogue which i guess is neat) ### Expectations 1. Should be factually correct 2. Should give a clear explanation (at least of similar clarity to the rule's source) 3. Should ideally be grammatically correct lol If you guys want me to open a main repo PR for this updating the documentation, I might have some time eventually. (Pls do ping though - i have a lot on my plate atm and reminders good) ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#7855.title: 📚 `noBannedTypes` has factually incorrect information; lacks depth

### Environment information

> biome rage

  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  false

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

After upgrading from 1.2.7 -> 1.3.0 and adding `css.parser.tailwindDirectives`, existing Tailwind CSS v4 `@plugin` code isn't parsed by biome.

# Support for `@plugin` w/ params existing in Tailwind CSS v4

It's practically undocumented. No consensus.

- https://tailwindcss.com/docs/functions-and-directives#plugin-directive
- https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/discussions/15997

It's tested in the codebase itself:

- https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/blob/v4.1.16/integrations/cli/plugins.test.ts#L108-L110

  ```css
  @plugin '@tailwindcss/forms' {
    strategy: base;
  }
  ```
- https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/blob/v4.1.16/integrations/upgrade/js-config.test.ts#L391-L406

  ```css
  @plugin '../custom-plugin' {
    is-null: null;
    is-true: true;
    is-false: false;
    is-int: 1234567;
    is-float: 1.35;
    is-sci: 0.0000135;
    is-str-null: 'null';
    is-str-true: 'true';
    is-str-false: 'false';
    is-str-int: '1234567';
    is-str-float: '1.35';
    is-str-sci: '1.35e-5';
    is-arr: 'foo', 'bar';
    is-arr-mixed: null, true, false, 1234567, 1.35, 'foo', 'bar', 'true';
  }
  ```
  
# Example on Biome Playground

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?cssModules=true&code=QABwAGwAdQBnAGkAbgAgACIAbQB5AC0AdABhAGkAbAB3AGkAbgBkAC0AcABsAHUAZwBpAG4AIgAgAHsACgAgACAALQAtAG0AeQAtAHYAYQByAGkAYQBiAGwAZQA6ACAAIgBGAGEAdgBvAHIAaQB0AGUAIAB2AGEAcgBpAGEAYgBsAGUAIgA7AAoAfQA%3D&language=css

# Example

```css
@plugin "my-tailwind-plugin" {
  --my-variable: "Favorite variable";
}
```

# Console output

```
/main.css:2:30 parse ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ expected `;` but instead found `{`
  
    1 │ /* Example 3: Only JavaScript with minimal shades for performance */
  > 2 │ @plugin "my-tailwind-plugin" {
      │                              ^
    3 │   --my-variable: "Favorite variable";

biomejs/biome#7861.body:
### Environment information

```block
CLI:
  Version:                      2.3.0
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             aarch64
  OS:                           macos

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         xterm-ghostty
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v22.12.0
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         pnpm/10.6.5

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

1.  Run `biome check` on a `.svelte` file that contains the following.

[Playground](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=PABzAGMAcgBpAHAAdAA%2BAAoAYwBvAG4AcwB0ACAAbgBhAG0AZQAgAD0AIAAiAG4AYQBtAGUAIgA7AAoAPAAvAHMAYwByAGkAcAB0AD4ACgAKADwAaQBuAHAAdQB0AAoAIAAgAHQAeQBwAGUAPQAiAHQAZQB4AHQAIgAKACAAIABpAGQAPQAiAG4AYQBtAGUAIgAKACAAIAB7AG4AYQBtAGUAfQAKACAAIAByAGUAcQB1AGkAcgBlAGQACgAvAD4A&language=svelte&ruleDomains.project=recommended)


```Svelte
<script>
     const name = "name";
</script>

<input
  type="text"
  id="name"
  {name}
  required
/>
```

2. The following output is observed:

```
Biome encountered an unexpected error

This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue.

When opening the issue, please provide a minimal reproduction, or identify and share the file/code that triggers it. Without a way to reproduce the error, the error can't be fixed:

Source Location: /Users/runner/work/biome/biome/crates/biome_parser/src/lib.rs:571:9
Thread Name: biome::workspace_worker_2
Message: The parser is no longer progressing. Stuck at '{' L_CURLY:96..97

✖ processing panicked: The parser is no longer progressing. Stuck at '{' L_CURLY:96..97

  ⚠ This diagnostic was derived from an internal Biome error. Potential bug, please report it if necessary.
```

3. Explicitly declaring the `name` attribute, and then running `biome check` resolves the issue.

```Svelte
<script>
     const name = "name";
</script>

<input
  type="text"
  id="name"
  name={name}
  required
/>
```

```json
// biome.json

{
  "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.3.0/schema.json",
  "html": {
  	"experimentalFullSupportEnabled": true,
  	"formatter": {
  		"enabled": true,
  		"indentScriptAndStyle": true
  	}
  },
  "overrides": [
  	{
  		"includes": ["**/*.svelte"],
  		"linter": {
  			"rules": {
  				"style": {
  					"useConst": "off",
  					"useImportType": "off"
  				},
  				"correctness": {
  					"noUnusedVariables": "off",
  					"noUnusedImports": "off"
  				}
  			}
  		}
  	}
  ],
}
```

Not sure if this would be considered separate to the exceptions mentioned here:
https://biomejs.dev/internals/language-support/#linting-html-ish-languages

Related Svelte docs:
https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/basic-markup#Element-attributes

### Expected result

The parser to progress, and no unexpected error on this file.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Rule name

lint/correctness/noUnusedImports

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lineWidth=120&indentStyle=space&lintRules=noUnusedImports&%3D%3D&language=ts

### Expected result

The lint rule shouldn't flag imports used in `@linkcode` docs the same way it doesn't flag imports used in `@link` docs.

https://jsdoc.app/tags-inline-link

While it's probably not used that much compared to the other two, `@linkplain` should be supported as well.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Expected result

The [Suppressions documentation](https://biomejs.dev/analyzer/suppressions/#suppression-syntax) suggests to use `// biome-ignore-start lint: <explanation>` and `// biome-ignore-end lint: <explanation>`, but this appears to have no effect on suppressing lint rule violations.

```typescript
// biome-ignore-start lint: explanation
const three = 3;
// biome-ignore-end lint: explanation
```

Expected: no `lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables` rule violation when surrounded by a range suppression for the `lint` category:

Actual: a `lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables` rule violation

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

suspicious/useIterableCallbackReturn suspicious/useNamespaceKeyword Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ``` ### Rule name Unknown ### Playground link https://github.com/klevercorrea/with-tailwindcss ### Expected result ### Bug Description I was testing Biome v2.3's new full support for Astro and its integration with Tailwind CSS v4. To do this, I created a fresh project using the official Astro `with-tailwindcss` template: ```bash pnpm create astro@latest -- --template with-tailwindcss ``` After setting up a basic Biome configuration on this new project, I discovered the following bug. --- When running `biome lint --write` on an `.astro` file, a "safe fix" from an unknown lint rule incorrectly adds a newline inside the frontmatter block. This action is not idempotent, and subsequent runs add more newlines. Crucially, running `biome lint` (without `--write`) on the same file reports no issues and suggests no fixes. This indicates a discrepancy where a fix is being applied by `--write` for an issue that isn't being detected by the linter on its own. ### Steps to Reproduce **1. `biome.json` configuration:** ```json { "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.3.2/schema.json", "linter": { "enabled": true, "rules": { "recommended": true } }, "html": { "experimentalFullSupportEnabled": true }, "overrides": [ { "includes": [ "**/*.astro" ], "linter": { "rules": { "correctness": { "noUnusedVariables": "off", "noUnusedImports": "off" } } } } ] } ``` **2. `example.astro` file:** ```astro --- const title = "Hello World"; --- {title}

{title}

``` **3. Commands to run:** First, run the linter without the write flag. ```bash # biome lint example.astro ``` > **Result:** Observe that it reports **no issues**. Next, run the linter with the `--write` flag. ```bash # biome lint --write example.astro ``` > **Result:** Observe that it reports that it **fixed the file**, and a newline is added to the frontmatter. Running it again adds another newline. ### Actual Behavior After running `biome lint --write example.astro` **once**, the file becomes: ```astro --- const title = "Hello World"; --- {title}

{title}

``` After running it a **second time**, it becomes: ```astro --- const title = "Hello World"; --- {title}

{title}

``` ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#7912.title: 💅 Linter: `lint --write` is not idempotent on Astro frontmatter, while `lint` reports no issues

biomejs/biome#7920.body:
### Environment information

```
playground
```

### Description

The CSS parser does not support the `prefix()` syntax when used with `@import` statements in Tailwind CSS v4.

Related to #7854

### Examples

```css
@import "tailwindcss/theme.css" layer(theme) prefix(tw);
@import "tailwindcss/utilities.css" prefix(tw) layer(utilities);
```

### Questions to address

- Does the order of `layer` and `prefix` matter?
- How does it interact with `supports()`?

### References

- Tailwind CSS documentation: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/preflight#disabling-preflight
- CSS Cascade spec: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-cascade/#at-import
- Original issue: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/7854
- Comment requesting this issue: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/7854#issuecomment-2558951398

biomejs/biome#7964.body:
### Environment information

```block
Biome v2.3.2
```

### What happened?

Biome is unable to parse this Tailwind custom variant:

```css
@custom-variant max-xxs (@media (max-width: 320px));
```

Getting this error:

> Expected a selector but instead found '@'.

But this is valid syntax according to the [Tailwind docs](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/adding-custom-styles#adding-custom-variants).

[**Playground link**](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?cssModules=true&code=QABjAHUAcwB0AG8AbQAtAHYAYQByAGkAYQBuAHQAIABtAGEAeAAtAHgAeABzACAAKABAAG0AZQBkAGkAYQAgACgAbQBhAHgALQB3AGkAZAB0AGgAOgAgADMAMgAwAHAAeAApACkAOwAgAC8AKgAgAC0ALQBiAHIAZQBhAGsAcABvAGkAbgB0AC0AeAB4AHMAIAAqAC8A&language=css)

### Expected result

Biome should be able to parse this Tailwind syntax.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#7994.body:
## Summary

I am currently working on issue #6725 to add support for the new CSS `if` feature and while doing so had to add new syntax kinds. In doing so though I realized that adding them will go over a current limit of how many syntax kinds are able to be supported by the parser.  Currently there are about [507 existing CSS kinds](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_css_syntax/src/generated/kind.rs#L7-L520) and the limit is [512](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_rowan/src/ast/mod.rs#L53). I wanted to split this out as it's own PR though, separate from the feature work since this is a deeper change to the parser and think it deserves more scrutiny. This PR just adds another u128 to the `SyntaxKindSet` struct array to increase the max syntax kinds to 640. 

## Test Plan

Full test suite of the repo passes

## Docs

n/a

## AI Assistance Disclosure

Claude Code was used to help locate where in the codebase the change needed to happen while working on the CSS `if` feature. The issue was originally surfaced by [this check](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_rowan/src/ast/mod.rs#L53) failing though.
biomejs/biome#7994.title: chore(parser): increase size of SyntaxKindSet bitfield

biomejs/biome#7999.body:
I used the playground to debug https://github.com/issues/assigned?issue=biomejs%7Cbiome%7C7332 and found a bug in the action log.

See console pane in [playground](https://next.biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noFloatingPromises&%3D).

```
    ℹ Unsafe fix: Add await operator.
  
    11 11 │     readonly.returnPromise();
    12 12 │     // ✅
    13    │ - ··object.returnPromise();
       13 │ + ··await·//·✅
       14 │ + ··object.returnPromise();
    14 15 │   }
    15 16 │
```

### Rule name

noFloatingPromises

### Playground link

[link](https://next.biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noFloatingPromises&%3D)

### Expected result

It should say

```
  ℹ Unsafe fix: Add await operator.
  
    11 11 │     readonly.returnPromise();
    12 12 │     // ✅
    13    │ - ··object.returnPromise();
       14 │ + ··await object.returnPromise();
    14 15 │   }
    15 16 │   
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Environment information ```bash CLI: Version: 2.3.3 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: aarch64 OS: macos Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset BIOME_THREADS: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: screen-256color JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: v22.20.0 JS_RUNTIME_NAME: node NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: unset Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Path: biome.json Formatter enabled: true Linter enabled: true Assist enabled: true VCS enabled: false Linter: JavaScript enabled: unset JSON enabled: unset CSS enabled: unset GraphQL enabled: unset Recommended: false Enabled rules: style/useConsistentCurlyBraces Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ``` ### Rule name lint/style/useConsistentCurlyBraces ### Playground link https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=useConsistentCurlyBraces&analyzerFixMode=safeAndUnsafeFixes&code=ZQB4AHAAbwByAHQAIABjAG8AbgBzAHQAIABDAG8AbQBwAG8AbgBlAG4AdAAgAD0AIAAoACkAIAA9AD4AIAAoAAoAIAAgADwAZABpAHYAPgAKACAAIAAgACAAewAiAHMAdABhAHIAdAAgAHsAewAiAH0ACgAgACAAIAAgADwAcwBwAGEAbgA%2BAFQARQBYAFQAPAAvAHMAcABhAG4APgAKACAAIAAgACAAewAiAH0AfQAgAGUAbgBkACIAfQAKACAAIAA8AC8AZABpAHYAPgAKACkAOwA%3D&ruleDomains.project=recommended ### Expected result As a result of `biome lint --write --unsafe` I get invalid JSX: I understand that it was "unsafe" but I did not expect to get invalid JSX as a result. I would expect Biome to keep characters that need escaping as JS expressions (in curly brackets): ```diff export const Comp = () => (
- {"start {{"} + start {"{{"} TEXT - {"}} end"} + {"}}"} end
); ``` Or do not highlight such strings as issues at all (to me it is still less destructive than the current behavior). ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#8027.body: ### Environment information ```bash CLI: Version: 2.3.3 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: aarch64 OS: macos Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset BIOME_THREADS: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: screen-256color JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: v22.20.0 JS_RUNTIME_NAME: node NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: unset Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Path: biome.json Formatter enabled: true Linter enabled: true Assist enabled: true VCS enabled: true Linter: JavaScript enabled: unset JSON enabled: unset CSS enabled: unset GraphQL enabled: unset Recommended: false Enabled rules: style/useReactFunctionComponents Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ``` ### Rule name lint/style/useReactFunctionComponents ### Playground link https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=useReactFunctionComponents&analyzerFixMode=safeAndUnsafeFixes&%3D%3D&ruleDomains.project=recommended ### Expected result When React class component is implemented with a class expression instead of a class statement, **useReactFunctionComponents** does not see `componentDidCatch` inside of it and reports an issue although documentation says it should not happen. So I expect this not to be an issue. FYI, if I convert my class expression to a class statement then Biome starts to notice `componentDidCatch` and reports no issues in this code: ```diff import { Component } from "react"; -const ErrorBoundary = class extends Component { +class ErrorBoundary extends Component { componentDidCatch(error, info) { console.log(error, info); } render() { return this.props.children; } } ``` ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8027.title: useReactFunctionComponents does not see `componentDidCatch` in class expressions

### Rule name

lint/style/noNestedTernary

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noNestedTernary&code=YwBvAG4AcwB0ACAAdgBhAGwAIAA9ACAAdAByAHUAZQAKAGMAbwBuAHMAdAAgAGMAYQBzAGUAMQAgAD0AIAB2AGEAbAAgAD8AIABNAGEAdABoAC4AcgBhAG4AZABvAG0AKAApACAAPwAgADEAIAA6ACAAMgA6ACAAMwA7AAoAYwBvAG4AcwB0ACAAYwBhAHMAZQAyACAAPQAgAHYAYQBsACAAPwAgADEAIAA6ACAATQBhAHQAaAAuAHIAYQBuAGQAbwBtACgAKQAgAD8AIAAyACAAOgAgADMAOwAKAA%3D%3D

### Expected result

### Expected Result
The `noNestedTernary` rule should detect nested ternary operators **regardless of whether parentheses are present**.

### Actual Behavior

When you check the Playground link, it appears to work correctly at first. However, if you copy the formatted output from the right panel (where Biome Formatter has added parentheses) and paste it back, **case1 is no longer detected as an error**.

This issue occurs in real-world scenarios when:
- Running `biome check` command
- Using editor Biome plugins with autofix enabled

### Comparison with ESLint

Notably, this issue doesn't occur with ESLint + Prettier:
- Prettier also adds parentheses in the same location for case1
- ESLint's `no-nested-ternary` rule still correctly detects the violation even with parentheses

### Reproduction Repository

For easier verification, I've created a reproduction repository that demonstrates the issue with both Biome and ESLint:
- https://github.com/fronterior/biome-no-nested-ternary-lint-issue



### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#8114.body:
### Description

### Description

https://orm.drizzle.team/docs/eslint-plugin

This tracks adding a new domain to biome for drizzle ORM code, mirroring their ESLint extension that has 2 rules:
- enforce-delete-with-where
- enforce-update-with-where

These rules prevent accidentally writing to an entire table due to forgetting a simple where clause.

Name suggestions:
* `noUpdateWithoutWhere`
* `noDeleteWithoutWhere`
* `useUpdateWithWhere`
* `useUpdateWithWhere`

**Want to contribute?** Let us know you are interested! We will assign you to the issue to prevent several people to work on the same issue. Don't worry, we can unassign you later if you are no longer interested in the issue! Read our [contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md?rgh-link-date=2025-10-02T22%3A39%3A36.000Z) and [analyzer contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_analyze/CONTRIBUTING.md?rgh-link-date=2025-10-02T22%3A39%3A36.000Z).
