biomejs/biome#8117.body:
### Environment information

```block
CLI:
  Version:                      2.3.5
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             aarch64
  OS:                           macos

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         xterm-256color
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v24.3.0
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         bun/1.3.2

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

The `useValidLang` rule incorrectly rejects valid BCP 47 language tags that include script subtags, such as `zh-Hans-CN` (Simplified Chinese as used in China).


### Expected result

According to [BCP 47](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/bcp/bcp47.txt) and [IANA Language Subtag Registry](https://www.iana.org/assignments/language-subtag-registry/language-subtag-registry), these tags should be valid:

```
<html lang="zh-Hans-CN" />  // ✅ Should be valid (Simplified Chinese, China)
<html lang="zh-Hant-TW" />  // ✅ Should be valid (Traditional Chinese, Taiwan)
<html lang="sr-Cyrl-RS" />  // ✅ Should be valid (Serbian, Cyrillic script, Serbia)
```

### Valid BCP 47 Language Tag Format

BCP 47 language tags follow this structure:
```
language [-script] [-region] [-variant] [-extension] [-privateuse]
```



## Root Cause
The current implementation in use_valid_lang.rs only supports the simple language-country format:
```rust
let mut split_value = attribute_text.split('-');
match (split_value.next(), split_value.next()) {
    (Some(language), Some(country)) => {
        // Validates as language-country only
        if !is_valid_language(language) { /* ... */ }
        else if !is_valid_country(country) { /* ... */ }
        else if split_value.next().is_some() {
            // Rejects any additional subtags
            return Some(UseValidLangState {
                invalid_kind: InvalidKind::Value,
            });
        }
    }
    // ...
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8117.title: 🐛 useValidLang rule incorrectly rejects valid BCP 47 language tags that include script subtags, such as zh-Hans-CN

### What happened?

If a function or a class member is accessed only from the constructor, it is incorrectly marked as unused. In the following code block the `usedMember` and `usedFunction` are marked as 'unused' although they are accessed from the constructor.

```typescript
export class ClassWithMembersUsedOnlyFromConstructor {
  private usedMember: string
  private otherMember: string

  constructor() {
    this.usedMember = ""
    this.usedFunction(this.usedMember);
  }

  private usedFunction(param: string) {
    console.log(param)
  }

  public alsoUsedFunction(): string {
    return this.otherMember;
  }
}
```

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%3D%3D

### Expected result

No lint errors should be reported.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8138.title: 🐛 Private class members used from constructor are incorrectly marked as unused


### Rule name

noSyncScripts

### Playground link

https://github.com/THernandez03/biome-noSyncScripts

### Why This is Incorrect

#### Module Scripts Are Already Deferred

According to the **[MDN Web Docs - JavaScript Modules](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Guide/Modules#other_differences_between_modules_and_classic_scripts)**:

> **3. Modules are deferred automatically**
> 
> Modules are automatically executed in defer mode. This means that:
> - Module scripts are downloaded in parallel to parsing the HTML
> - Module scripts are executed after the document has been parsed
> - The relative order of multiple module scripts is preserved

### Expected result

The `noSyncScripts` rule should be updated to:

1. **Skip validation** for `<script>` tags with `type="module"`
2. Only flag classic `<script>` tags without `defer` or `async` attributes
3. Optionally provide an informational message explaining that module scripts are already deferred

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8144.title: 💅 Improve noSyncScripts rule

> - PRs must go to the `next` branch, with a `minor` changeset
> - You have to comment if you're working on a rule. This will avoid having multiple contributors working on the same rule. In case of double PRs, precedence will go to users that comment here

> [!NOTE]
> - When working on a rule, you also need to consider `.astro`, `.vue` and `.svelte` files. These files might have their own syntax and nodes. Refer to the [`html.ungram`](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/xtask/codegen/html.ungram) file 
> - When checking element names, the check must be case insensitive in `.html` files, but case sensitive in `.astro`, `.vue` and `.svelte` files. This is because of how components work in those languages.
> - We still don't have a way to query variables and components exposed by the JavaScript embedded code. If you happen to need that information, please consider making the rule more lax and adding this to the documentation using the following syntax
>   ```md
>   :::note
>   Explanation here...  
>   :::
>   ```

biomejs/biome#8179.body:
## Environment information

```bash
CLI:
  Version:                      2.3.6
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             aarch64
  OS:                           macos

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         xterm-256color
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v22.21.1
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         pnpm/9.13.2

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
```

## Rule name

`nursery/useConsistentArrowReturn`

## Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=useConsistentArrowReturn&files.main.tsx=YwBvAG4AcwB0ACAAZwBlAHQAUwBjAGgAZQBtAGEAUgBvAHcAVAB5AHAAZQBzACAAPQAgACgAbAA6ACAAcwB0AHIAaQBuAGcAKQAgAD0APgAKACAAIABsAAoAIAAgACAAIAAuAHMAcABsAGkAdAAoACIAXABuACIAKQA%3D&files.biome.%3D%3D

## Expected result

### Current Behavior (Bug)

When running `biome lint --fix` via CLI with the `useConsistentArrowReturn` rule configured with `style: "always"`, the autofix produces **semantically incorrect code** that changes the function's behavior.

**Input code:**
```typescript
const foo = (l: string) =>
  l
    .split('\n')
```

**CLI autofix output (INCORRECT):**
```typescript
const foo = (l: string) =>
  {
  return
  l
    .split("\n");
}
```

**Problem:** The function now returns `undefined` instead of the split array because there's a newline after `return`, triggering JavaScript's automatic semicolon insertion (ASI). This silently breaks working code by changing its runtime behavior.

### Expected Behavior

The autofix should produce:

```typescript
const foo = (l: string) => {
  return l.split('\n');
};
```

This is what VS Code's Quick Fix (`source.fixAll.biome`) correctly produces when applying the same lint rule fix.

### Configuration Used

```json
{
  "linter": {
    "rules": {
      "nursery": {
        "useConsistentArrowReturn": {
          "level": "warn",
          "options": { "style": "always" }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Save the input code to a file (e.g., `test.ts`)
2. Run: `biome lint --fix test.ts`
3. Observe the incorrect output with `return` on a separate line from the value

### Impact

This bug can **silently break working code** by changing its runtime behavior:
- The function returns `undefined` instead of the intended value
- The code remains syntactically valid, so the error may not be caught until runtime
- This is a critical semantic change that could cause production bugs

### Additional Context

- VS Code's Quick Fix works correctly, suggesting the issue is specific to the CLI autofix implementation
- According to Discord discussion, VS Code likely runs format after applying the fix, which corrects the incorrect return placement
- The CLI autofix should produce correct code without requiring a subsequent format pass
- This was discussed in the Biome Discord where maintainer Siketyan confirmed this is a bug
biomejs/biome#8179.title: 🐛 useConsistentArrowReturn autofix produces semantically incorrect code (returns undefined)

Rules:

```json
"noParameterAssign": {
  "level": "error",
  "options": { "propertyAssignment": "deny" }
}
```

Output:

```javascript
function f(a) {
	const b = { c: 0 };

	b.c = a; // Assigning to a property of a function parameter is confusing.
	b.c = 1;
}
```

### Expected result

### Rule name

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=KgAgAHsACgAgACAACQBmAG8AbgB0AC0AZgBhAG0AaQBsAHkAOgAgACIATgBvAHQAbwAgAFMAZQByAGkAZgAiACwAIAB2AGEAcgAoAC0ALQBzAGUAcgBpAGYAKQA7AAoAfQA%3D&language=css

### Expected result

As [documetation](https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-generic-font-names/#description) states, this rule should be ignored with situation *The last value being a CSS variable*.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Rule name

lint/correctness/noUnknownProperty

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=QABpAG0AcABvAHIAdAAgACIAdABhAGkAbAB3AGkAbgBkAGMAcwBzACIAOwAKAEAAcABsAHUAZwBpAG4AIAAiAEAAaQBjAG8AbgBpAGYAeQAvAHQAYQBpAGwAdwBpAG4AZAA0ACIAIAB7AAoACQBwAHIAZQBmAGkAeABlAHMAOgAgAG0AZABpAAoAfQA7AAoA&language=css

### Expected result

Tailwind CSS v4 [introduced a syntax](https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/pull/14264) allowing options to be passed to plugins directly in CSS files via a declaration block.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8340.title: 💅 Support Tailwind v4 `@plugin` options syntax

### Rule name

noUnusedVariables

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noUnusedVariables&%3D%3D

### Expected result

```ts
const obj = { ignore1: true, ignore2: true, rest1: true, rest2: true };
const { ignore1, ignore2, ...rest } = obj;
console.log(rest);
```

Expect no error if `ignoreRestSiblings` actually defaulted to `true`, as indicated in the [docs](https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-unused-variables/#options).

Docs state:

> #### ignoreRestSiblings
> Whether to ignore unused variables from an object destructuring with a spread (i.e.: a and b in `const { a, b, ...rest } = obj` should be ignored by this rule).
> 
> Defaults to true.

Error stops occurring if this config is added, again implying `ignoreRestSiblings` actually defaults to `false`.

```
"noUnusedVariables": { "level": "error", "options": { "ignoreRestSiblings": true } }
```

In my opinion, it *should* default to true as indicated in the docs, as spreading in this manner is common practice for excluding properties from an object.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8353.title: 💅 noUnusedVariables ignoreRestSiblings option defaults to false, contradicting docs

biomejs/biome#8355.body:
### Description

We should port over the [`ignoreDifferentlyNamedParameters`](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/unified-signatures/#ignoredifferentlynamedparameters) and [`ignoreOverloadsWithDifferentJSDoc`](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/unified-signatures/#ignoreoverloadswithdifferentjsdoc) options from the source rule. I don't really have any strong feelings about the naming for these options, even though they are a bit verbose. Makes some sense to keep the same option names.

Discussed in #7218

Since these are new options, they have to go in a minor release. Please make sure to make your PR against the `next` branch.

biomejs/biome#8360.body:
### Environment information

```block
CLI:
  Version:                      2.2.5
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             aarch64
  OS:                           macos

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         xterm-256color
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v20.19.3
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         pnpm/10.16.0

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         /Users/jleupin/app-components/biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  false

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

When using extends: "//" to extend from a parent biome.json, GritQL plugins defined in the child configuration don't execute against files in that package.

Environment
Biome version: 2.2.5

OS: macOS

Reproduction

Monorepo structure:

```
/
├── biome.json          # root config
└── packages/
    └── mobile/
        ├── biome.json  # extends from root
        └── biome-plugins/
            └── my-plugin.grit
```

Root `biome.json`:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.2.5/schema.json",
  "linter": {
    "enabled": true
  }
}
```

Child `packages/mobile/biome.json`:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.2.5/schema.json",
  "extends": "//",
  "plugins": ["./biome-plugins/my-plugin.grit"],
  "linter": {
    "enabled": true,
    "rules": {
      "recommended": true
    }
  }
}
```

Plugin `packages/mobile/biome-plugins/my-plugin.grit`

```
`$x as unknown as $y` where {
    register_diagnostic(
        span = $x,
        message = "Found pattern",
        severity = "warn"
    )
}
```


### Expected result

**Expected Behavior**

Running biome lint src/some-file.tsx from packages/mobile/ should execute the plugin and report diagnostics.

**Actual Behavior**

The plugin loads without error, but no diagnostics are reported. Running biome search with the same pattern finds matches, but biome lint doesn't trigger the plugin.

**Additional Notes**

- The plugin file is readable and the path resolves correctly (no "Cannot read file" error)
- biome search with the same GritQL pattern finds matches
- Files outside the workspace (e.g., /tmp/test.ts) work correctly with the plugin
- Only files within the package that uses extends are affected

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8360.title: 🐛 Plugins defined in child config don't work when using extends: "//"

biomejs/biome#8363.body:
## Environment - **Biome version**: 2.3.8 - **Node.js version**: v25.2.1 - **OS**: macOS Darwin 25.1.0 (darwin-arm64) ## Description When running `biome check` on HTML files, Biome crashes with an internal error when it encounters a `<` character followed by a number in text content (e.g., `<12 months`). While Biome correctly identifies this as a parse error (it expects `<` to start a tag), the internal handling causes a thread panic with `HTML_BOGUS` cast failure. ## Error Message ``` Biome encountered an unexpected error This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue. Source Location: /Users/runner/work/biome/biome/crates/biome_rowan/src/ast/mod.rs:206:13 Thread Name: biome::workspace_worker_0 Message: Tried to cast node with kind HTML_BOGUS as "biome_html_syntax::generated::nodes::HtmlRoot" but was unable to cast ``` ## Minimal Reproduction Create a file `test.html`: ```html Test
Target <12 months
``` Run: ```bash npx @biomejs/biome check test.html ``` The parse error is expected (Biome interprets `<12` as an invalid tag start), but the crash with `HTML_BOGUS` cast failure should not occur. ## Expected Behavior Biome should report the parse error gracefully without crashing. The error message for the parse issue is fine: ``` test.html:7:17 parse ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ × Expected an attribute but instead found '<'. > 7 │
Target <12 months
│ ^ ``` But after reporting this, it should continue processing without the internal `HTML_BOGUS` cast failure. ## Additional Context - The error occurred multiple times (4 times in my case) when running on a directory with multiple HTML files containing similar patterns - No `biome.json` configuration file was present (default settings) - The `<` followed by a number pattern (like `<12`) is common in content discussing metrics, comparisons, or mathematical expressions
biomejs/biome#8363.title: HTML parser crash: HTML_BOGUS cast failure on '<' character in text content

biomejs/biome#8402.body:
### Description

Starlight allows rendering markdown inside a warning/caution/info block:
```mdx
:::caution
Biome doesn't check what's changed, this means that even adding spaces or newlines to a file, will mark this file as "changed"
:::
```

We should mention this as it is quite useful when writing rust docs for linter rules.
biomejs/biome#8402.title: 📎 Add mention of starlight "alert" blocks to analyzer CONTRIBUTING.md

``` ### Rule name noSvgWithoutTitle ### Playground link https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lineWidth=120&lintRules=noSvgWithoutTitle&code=PAA%2BAAoAPABzAHYAZwA%2BAAoACQA8AHIAZQBjAHQAIAAvAD4ACgAJADwAcgBlAGMAdAAgAC8APgAKAAkAPABnAD4ACgAJAAkAPABjAGkAcgBjAGwAZQAgAC8APgAKAAkACQA8AGMAaQByAGMAbABlACAALwA%2BAAoACQAJADwAZwA%2BAAoACQAJAAkAPAB0AGkAdABsAGUAPgBQAGEAcwBzADwALwB0AGkAdABsAGUAPgAKAAkACQAJADwAYwBpAHIAYwBsAGUAIAAvAD4ACgAJAAkACQA8AGMAaQByAGMAbABlACAALwA%%2BAA%3D%3D ### Expected result When I worked on porting this rule from JSX to HTML, I found two problems. https://github.com/biomejs/biome/pull/8412 Maybe I'll fix them by myself. 1. `` element should not be in the descendant of other svg elements such as `<g>` for the spec of [SVG 1.1](https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/struct.html#DescriptionAndTitleElements), so [the first Valid case](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/pull/8412/files#diff-12b71a5df08d3440f897fd00650536571fefb9e4d920731872a1a4407ed24fe9R48-R62) should report diagnostic. For the spec of SVG2, this is not applicable but [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Reference/Element/title) mentions this from the point of backward compatibility.>Note: For backward compatibility with SVG 1.1, <title> elements should be the first child element of their parent. 2. `<svg role="graphics-symbol"><rect /></svg>` and `<svg role="graphics-symbol img"><rect /></svg>` should report diagnostics because they don't have accessible name. [WAI-ARIA Graphics Module](https://www.w3.org/TR/2018/REC-graphics-aria-1.0-20181002/#graphics-symbol) says `graphics-symbol` role requires accessible name, and of course `img` role does.Their cases are handled in the code `_ => None,`, so we should handle them with match arms. axe checks `<img>`, `graphics-document` role, and `graphics-symbol` role, `noSvgWithoutTitle` should also support them. https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.10/svg-img-alt ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### What happened?

1. Enable the formatter
2. Override linter settings for a file type
3. Try to format that file type

```json
{
  "formatter": {
    "enabled": true
  },
  "overrides": [
    {
      "includes": ["**/*.vue"],
      "linter": {
        "enabled": false
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Expected result

The formatter setting is undisturbed, inheriting the global setting. Instead, one must re-set it to `enabled` in the override.

```block
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         npm/10.9.3

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.jsonc
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               false
  VCS enabled:                  false

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

When using `html:active-view-transition-type(forwards, backwards) {...}` from the [MDN example](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Selectors/:active-view-transition-type) (also syntax mentioned in the [spec](https://www.w3.org/TR/css-view-transitions-2/#the-active-view-transition-type-pseudo)), the following error is displayed: `Expected an identifier but instead found ','.`

[Repro in Playground](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=aAB0AG0AbAA6AGEAYwB0AGkAdgBlAC0AdgBpAGUAdwAtAHQAcgBhAG4AcwBpAHQAaQBvAG4ALQB0AHkAcABlACgAZgBvAHIAdwBhAHIAZABzACwAIABiAGEAYwBrAHcAYQByAGQAcwApACAAewAKACAAIAAvACoAIAAuAC4ALgAgACoALwAKAH0A&language=css)

### Expected result

No error

### Rule name

lint/suspicious/noExtraNonNullAssertion

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%3D

### Expected result

Biome v2.3.8 and previous would permit an expression with chained non-null assertions, but v2.3.9 is marking these assertions as extras.

```ts
// from playground:
const _d2 = blob.b!.d!.length;
```

Applying the safe fix from Biome in this case will cause TypeScript to fail when the `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` flag is set in `tsconfig.json`.

```
> tsc --noEmit

test.ts:12:13 - error TS18048: 'blob.b' is possibly 'undefined'.

12 const _d2 = blob.b.d!.length;
               ~~~~~~
```

For reference, here are the config files I'm using locally:

<details><summary>biome.json</summary>

```json
{
    "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.3.9/schema.json",
    "linter": {
        "enabled": true,
        "rules": {
            "recommended": true
        }
    }
}
```

</details>

<details><summary>package.json</summary>

```json
{
    "name": "biome-bug",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "description": "",
    "main": "test.ts",
    "scripts": {
        "lint": "biome check test.ts",
        "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
    },
    "keywords": [],
    "author": "",
    "license": "ISC",
    "packageManager": "pnpm@10.18.2",
    "dependencies": {
        "@biomejs/biome": "2.3.9",
        "typescript": "^5.9.3"
    }
}
```

</details>

<details><summary>tsconfig.json</summary>

```json
{
    "compilerOptions": {
        "target": "ESNext",
        "strict": true,
        "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
        "skipLibCheck": true
    },
    "include": ["test.ts"]
}
```

</details>



### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#8488.body:
### Environment information

```block
CLI:
  Version:                      2.3.9
  Color support:                false

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             aarch64
  OS:                           macos

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         unset
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v22.16.0
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         unset

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

When using a relative path for a GritQL plugin in the root biome.json, Biome fails with "Cannot read file" error during configuration loading. Absolute paths work correctly. Steps to reproduce
Create a monorepo with a root biome.json:
```
{
    "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.3.9/schema.json",
    "root": true,
    "plugins": ["./biome/my-plugin.grit"]
}
```
Create a plugin file at ./biome/my-plugin.grit:
```
engine biome(1.0)
language js(typescript, jsx)

`new Date($...)` as $expr where {
    register_diagnostic(span=$expr, message="Avoid new Date", severity="warn")
}
```
Run ```biome check .```
Expected result: Plugin loads and diagnostics are reported. 
Actual result:

  ✖ Error(s) during loading of plugins:
    Cannot read file.


  ✖ Biome exited because the configuration resulted in errors. Please fix them.

Workaround: Using an absolute path works:
```
{
    "plugins": ["/absolute/path/to/biome/my-plugin.grit"]
}
```
Notes
The plugin file exists and has correct read permissions
This is different from #8360, which was about child configs with extends: "//"
The [documentation](https://biomejs.dev/linter/plugins/) shows "./path-to-plugin.grit" as the expected format

### Expected result

Plugin loads successfully and reports diagnostics when running biome check ., biome lint ., or any other Biome command.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8488.title: 🐛 Plugin relative paths fail with "Cannot read file" when loading configuration

### Description

When using [noUndedclaredEnvVars](https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-undeclared-env-vars/), it should also track/capture cases when using `Bun.env` and string accessed environment variables (i.e. `process.env["MY_VAR"]` instead of `process.env.MY_VAR`).

### What happened?

1. Use a monorepo structure
2. Configure a root config that has some global `files.includes` exclusions via `!`
3. Configure a monorepo package configuration that uses `"extends": "//"`
4. Create some lint failures of an enabled rule in the excluded files
5. Run lint
6. See lint errors for excluded files

https://github.com/brandonw/biome-repro-1766175331469 has a somewhat minimal reproduction.

### Expected result

Excluded files should not be processed.

### Code of Conduct

### Environment information

```block

  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  false
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

I use Biome to lint and format a Svelte component library: https://github.com/carbon-design-system/carbon-components-svelte

Repro: https://github.com/carbon-design-system/carbon-components-svelte/pull/2439

When upgrading to Biome 2.3.7 or later, I experience a benign diagnostic, which flags [`TreeViewNode.svelte`](https://github.com/carbon-design-system/carbon-components-svelte/blob/master/src/TreeView/TreeViewNode.svelte):

```sh
$ biome check --write .
  ⚠ Biome encountered an unusually large amount of types which exceeded the limit of 200,000.
    
    Either you are analyzing very large files (did you make sure to exclude your build/ or dist/ folder?), or you've encountered a bug in Biome.
    
    Please follow these instructions to discover if you are accidentally analyzing large files and what to do about them in the relative guide.
  
  ℹ In the meantime, you can force this file to be ignored using a `!!` pattern in the files.includes option in your configuration file.
  
  ℹ Refer to the documentation for more information.
  
  ℹ If you think this is a bug, please report it and include the following information:
  
  - source code of the file;
  - how the file is imported in the project (by a test file, a dependency, etc.);
  - if and how the file/folder is excluded.
  
  ⚠ Failing to provide this information won't allow the team to fix the issue.
  
  ⚠ This diagnostic was derived from an internal Biome error. Potential bug, please report it if necessary.
  

Checked 1059 files in 585ms. No fixes applied.
```

Through trial and error, the diagnostic disappears when commenting out the following code in the Svelte context script:

This diagnostic disappears when I comment out the following line:

```diff
<script context="module">
  export function computeTreeLeafDepth(node) {
    let depth = 0;

    if (node == null) return depth;

    let parentNode = node.parentNode;

    while (parentNode != null && parentNode.getAttribute("role") !== "tree") {
-     parentNode = parentNode.parentNode;
      if (parentNode.tagName === "LI") depth++;
    }

    return depth;
  }
</script>
```

This diagnostic is not reproducible in the Biome playground, since it seems related to the config `files.includes`.



### Expected result

It should not print out the benign diagnostic warning message.

Biome 2.3.6: no error
Biome 2.3.7: with error
Biome 2.3.10: with error

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8527.title: 🐛 Linting Svelte context module produces "unusually large amount of types" diagnostic since Biome 2.3.7

No

biomejs/biome#8565.body:
### Description

I saw in the [vue 3.6 beta patch notes](https://github.com/vuejs/core/releases/tag/v3.6.0-beta.1) that there's new options to opt in to using their new vapor mode:

```vue
<script setup vapor>
// ...
</script>
```

We should add a HTML lint rule to let users enforce using this in their codebases.

Name suggestions:
- `useVueVapor`

This should go in the Vue domain, and **not** be recommended (since vapor is still unstable).

**Want to contribute?** Let us know you are interested! We will assign you to the issue to prevent several people to work on the same issue. Don't worry, we can unassign you later if you are no longer interested in the issue! Read our [contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [analyzer contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_analyze/CONTRIBUTING.md).
biomejs/biome#8565.title: 📎 New lint rule to enforce using `vapor` in Vue SFC

biomejs/biome#8566.body:
### Description

In the [vue 3.6 beta release notes](https://github.com/vuejs/core/releases/tag/v3.6.0-beta.1), it says that the options API is not usable in vapor mode.

We should add a lint rule that prohibits using the options API to aid users who want to transition to vapor mode.

Name suggestions:
- `noVueOptionsApi`

This should go in the Vue domain, and not recommended

**Want to contribute?** Let us know you are interested! We will assign you to the issue to prevent several people to work on the same issue. Don't worry, we can unassign you later if you are no longer interested in the issue! Read our [contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [analyzer contributing guide](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/main/crates/biome_analyze/CONTRIBUTING.md).
biomejs/biome#8566.title: 📎 New lint rule to ban Vue options API

biomejs/biome#8590.body:
### Description

Right now, users are unable to use these very valuable rules because they currently don't function across language boundaries:
- `noUnusedVariables`
- `noUnusedImports`
- `useImportType`
- `useConst`

All of these rules rely on the semantic model to feed them the correct info. However, its currently confined to just javascript, and is unable to inspect the contents of the parent HTML-ish language to continue building the semantic model.
biomejs/biome#8590.title: 📎 Support for cross language lint rules (`noUnusedVariables`, `noUnusedImports`, etc)

### What happened?

Here's a simple example repo to reproduce this bug: https://github.com/brianhelba/biome-panic

---
The output from this is:
```
> biome check

Biome encountered an unexpected error

This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue.

When opening the issue, please provide a minimal reproduction, or identify and share the file/code that triggers it. Without a way to reproduce the error, the error can't be fixed:

Source Location: /__w/biome/biome/crates/biome_rowan/src/ast/mod.rs:206:13
Thread Name: biome::workspace_worker_0
Message: Tried to cast node with kind HTML_BOGUS as `"biome_html_syntax::generated::nodes::HtmlRoot"` but was unable to cast

 at src/django.html internalError/panic  INTERNAL  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ processing panicked: Tried to cast node with kind HTML_BOGUS as `"biome_html_syntax::generated::nodes::HtmlRoot"` but was unable to cast
  
  ⚠ This diagnostic was derived from an internal Biome error. Potential bug, please report it if necessary.
```

### Expected result

Biome should not panic, even when parsing unsupported (Django template syntax) files.

biomejs/biome#8645.body:
### Environment information

```block
CLI:
  Version:                      2.3.9
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             aarch64
  OS:                           macos

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset
  BIOME_CONFIG_PATH:            unset
  BIOME_THREADS:                unset
  NO_COLOR:                     unset
  TERM:                         xterm-256color
  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v25.2.1
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         bun/1.3.5

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

### What happened?

Consider the following code:

```ts
async function* plus(iterable: AsyncIterable<number>, n: number) {
  for await (const v of iterable) {
    yield v + n;
  }
}

function* plusThreeEverythingAndThenPlusTwoEverything(
  iterable: AsyncIterable<number>,
) {
  yield* plus(iterable, 3);
  yield* plus(iterable, 2);
}
```
https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%2BACwACgApACAAewAKACAAIAB5AGkAZQBsAGQAKgAgAHAAbAB1AHMAKABpAHQAZQByAGEAYgBsAGUALAAgADMAKQA7AAoAIAAgAHkAaQBlAGwAZAAqACAAcABsAHUAcwAoAGkAdABlAHIAYQBiAGwAZQAsACAAMgApADsACgB9AAoA

EDIT: the code above is a bit silly because the second `plus(…)` call doesn't do anything — the iterator is already consumed. See [below](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8645#issuecomment-3706039224) for an example that can't be trivially rewritten.

Biome errors and tells me that the `async` keyword is unnecessary:

> Remove this async modifier, or add an await expression in the function. Async functions without await expressions may not need to be declared async.

However, removing the `async` would cause the code to break, because we are yielding to an `async` iterable.

### Expected result

No error.

I'm not sure exactly what the best course of action here is. The simplest would be to count `yield*` similar to an `await` call in an `async` function — that's probably what I would do to eliminate false positives. But `yield* …` can also be used to yield to sync generators (even from inside `async` functions), so this would allow false negatives.[^1]

[^1]: Conceptually, using `yield*` exclusively on known-sync generators in an `async` function feels similar to `await`ing a non-thenable, but TypeScript tends to warn in that case. To report proper diagnostics, Biome would have to know the types of all the iterables that `await*` is used on.

However, the code is concise and idiomatic for combinators of iterables. It doesn't feel right to rewrite it or to add a permanent comment for Biome to ignore it.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8645.title: 🐛 Linter errors and suggests removing `async` when doing so would break a `yield*` call

### Environment information

```bash
CLI:

  Color support:                true

Platform:
```

```bash
  OS:                           macos

  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         npm/11.6.2

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0


```

</details>


### What happened?

When creating a utility with a parameter (`-*`) in Tailwind v4, Biome's parser reports the error:
```
  ✖ expected `{` but instead found `-`

    160 │ 
  > 161 │ @utility px-* {
        │            ^
    162 │       padding-right: --value(--s-x-*);
    163 │       padding-left: --value(--s-x-*);
    164 | }
```
Escaping the characters with backslashes stops Biome from reporting the error, but breaks Tailwind.

Code:
```css
@theme {
	--w-topic-card: 20rem;
}

/* Define utilities to match custom values */
@utility w-* {
	width: --value(--w-*);
}
```

I've added the `tailwindDirectives` to my biome.json file without success.


### Expected result

Should not report an error

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Environment information

<details>

```bash
biome rage
CLI:
  Version:                      2.3.11
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             x86_64
  OS:                           linux

Environment:
  BIOME_LOG_PATH:               unset
  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset

  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v22.21.1
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         unset

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

</details>


### What happened?

1. Have an `@apply` as the last statement inside a block (e.g. `@utility container`)
2. Do not terminate the statement with `;`
3. See the error `expected ; but instead the file ends` but AFTER the closing bracket of the block. Prettier is able to correctly format this.

e.g. 
```css
@utility container {
  @apply mx-auto space-y-4 px-8 py-10 sm:px-16 md:px-24 lg:px-32
}
```
See https://next.biomejs.dev/playground/?cssModules=true&code=QAB1AHQAaQBsAGkAdAB5ACAAYwBvAG4AdABhAGkAbgBlAHIAIAB7AAoAIAAgAEAAYQBwAHAAbAB5ACAAbQB4AC0AYQB1AHQAbwAgAHMAcABhAGMAZQAtAHkALQA0ACAAcAB4AC0AOAAgAHAAeQAtADEAMAAgAHMAbQA6AHAAeAAtADEANgAgAG0AZAA6AHAAeAAtADIANAAgAGwAZwA6AHAAeAAtADMAMgAKAH0ACgA%3D&language=css

If I were to manually "correct" the errors highlighted by the biome parser, biome would accept the input, but no longer prettier, because there would be more closing brackets than opening ones. It seems like biome mangles the closing bracket of the block into the `@apply` statement.
e.g.
```css
@utility container {
  @apply mx-auto space-y-4 px-8 py-10 sm:px-16 md:px-24 lg:px-32
}
;}
```
See https://next.biomejs.dev/playground/?cssModules=true&code=QAB1AHQAaQBsAGkAdAB5ACAAYwBvAG4AdABhAGkAbgBlAHIAIAB7AAoAIAAgAEAAYQBwAHAAbAB5ACAAbQB4AC0AYQB1AHQAbwAgAHMAcABhAGMAZQAtAHkALQA0ACAAcAB4AC0AOAAgAHAAeQAtADEAMAAgAHMAbQA6AHAAeAAtADEANgAgAG0AZAA6AHAAeAAtADIANAAgAGwAZwA6AHAAeAAtADMAMgAKAH0ACgA7AH0A&language=css

### Expected result

In both cases, prettier seems to be right:

```css
@utility container {
	@apply mx-auto space-y-4 px-8 py-10 sm:px-16 md:px-24 lg:px-32;
}
```
and
```css
CssSyntaxError: Unexpected } (4:2)
  2 |   @apply mx-auto space-y-4 px-8 py-10 sm:px-16 md:px-24 lg:px-32
  3 | }
> 4 | ;}
    |  ^
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Environment information

<details>

```bash

CLI:

  Color support:                true

Platform:

  OS:                           linux

  JS_RUNTIME_VERSION:           v20.19.6
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         npm/10.8.2

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  false
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0

```

</details>


### What happened?

I've noticed that if `biome lint` or `biome check` are run with `--reporter=summary` then some diagnostic counts get inappropriately attributed to the wrong rule. 

Codesandbox link: https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/clever-colden-9fq8xk
I added a "summary" task to reproduce the issue.

In my example I have a test file that has 8 issues (4 noUnusedVariables, 1 noGlobalIsNan, and 3 noExplicitAny).

The issue is that given this test file, the summary reporter outputs this:
```bash
  Rule Name                                        Diagnostics
  
  lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables               4 (4 warnings)
  lint/suspicious/noGlobalIsNan                         4 (4 warnings)
```
which for some reason is grouping the noExplicitAny under noGlobalIsNan. 

I also tested out changing the noExplicitAny rule to the "info" level, which resulted in this summary:
```bash
  Rule Name                                        Diagnostics
  
  lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables               4 (4 warnings)
  lint/suspicious/noGlobalIsNan                         4 (1 warning, 3 infos)
```
Which is why I'm confident the 3 extra diagnostics attributed to noGlobalIsNan are from noExplicitAny. 








### Expected result

I would expect the summary output to be something like this
```bash
  lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables               4 (4 warnings)
  lint/suspicious/noGlobalIsNan                         1 (1 warning)
  lint/suspicious/noExplicitAny                          3 (3 warnings)
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

```bash
Biome encountered an unexpected error

This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue.

When opening the issue, please provide a minimal reproduction, or identify and share the file/code that triggers it. Without a way to reproduce the error, the error can't be fixed:

Source Location: /__w/biome/biome/crates/biome_parser/src/diagnostic.rs:395:14
Thread Name: biome::workspace_worker_8
Message: Expected token to be a punctuation or keyword.

admin/src/components/input/Time.vue internalError/panic  INTERNAL  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ processing panicked: Expected token to be a punctuation or keyword.
```

### Environment information

Playground.

### Configuration

```JSON

```

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?indentStyle=space&quoteProperties=preserve&indentWidth=4&arrowParentheses=as-needed&enabledLinting=false&enabledAssist=false&code=dAB5AHAAZQAgAEEAPABCACwAIABDACAAZQB4AHQAZQBuAGQAcwAgAEQAPgAgAD0ACgAgACAALwAvACAAUwBvAG0AZQAgAGMAbwBtAG0AZQBuAHQACgAgACAAdQBuAGQAZQBmAGkAbgBlAGQAOwA%3D&language=ts

### Expectation

I would expect it to be formatted like Prettier:

```typescript
type A<B, C extends D> =
    // Some comment
    undefined;
```

Instead the continuation is not indented:
```typescript
type A<B, C extends D> = // Some comment
undefined;
```

If the `extends` is dropped, it's indented:

```typescript
type A<B, C> =
    // Some comment
    undefined;
```

Biome should not reference `day` in its dependency. `day` only exists within the `Array.prototype.map` function's scope and does not exist outside.

### Rule name

lint/suspicious/noArrayIndexKey

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noArrayIndexKey&%3D%3D

### Expected result

Good scenario - correctly reports an error as the `key` is relying on the `index` of the array:
```JSX
<div key={`${item}-${index}`}>
``` 

Edge case - when rearranging the order of the values in the key, we should still get an error but we don't:
```JSX
<div key={`${index}-${item}`}>
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8812.title: 💅  lint/suspicious/noArrayIndexKey false negative when re-ordering value and index in template string

```bash
  JS_RUNTIME_NAME:              node
  NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER:         npm/11.6.2

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  true

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

</details>


### What happened?

GritQL patterns for named exports (`export { $foo } from $source`) fail to match export statements, while the structurally similar star export pattern (`export * from $source`) works correctly.

### Reproduction steps

1. Create a directory and navigate to it:
```bash
mkdir -p biome_repro && cd biome_repro
```

2. Create a GritQL rule file (`rule.grit`):
```grit
`export { $foo } from $source` where {
  $source <: r"^['\"]\.\.?/.*",
  register_diagnostic(span=$source, message="Found relative export")
}
```

3. Create `biome.json`:
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.3.11/schema.json",
  "plugins": ["./rule.grit"],
  "linter": { "enabled": true, "rules": { "recommended": false } }
}
```

4. Create a test file (`repro.ts`):
```typescript
export { foo } from "../../deep/path";
```

5. Run Biome lint:
```bash
npx @biomejs/biome lint repro.ts
```

**Result**: No diagnostic is emitted. ❌

### Comparison with working patterns

| Pattern | Code | Result |
|---------|------|--------|
| `export * from $source` | `export * from "../../deep/path";` | ✅ Matches |
| `import $name from $source` | `import foo from "../../deep/path";` | ✅ Matches |
| `export { $foo } from $source` | `export { foo } from "../../deep/path";` | ❌ No match |

### Unit test reproduction (Biome source)

I verified this bug in the Biome source code by adding a test to `crates/biome_grit_patterns/tests/quick_test.rs`:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_export_named_from_deep_path() {
    let parse_grit_result = parse_grit(
        r#"`export { $foo } from $source` where {
            $source <: r"^['\"]\.\.?/.*"
        }"#,
    );
    // ... setup code ...

    let body = r#"export { foo } from "../../deep/path";"#;
    let parsed = parse(body, JsFileSource::js_module(), JsParserOptions::default());
    let file = GritTargetFile::new("test.js", parsed.into());
    let GritQueryResult { effects, .. } = query.execute(file).expect("could not execute query");

    // THIS FAILS - effects is empty
    assert!(!effects.is_empty(), "Expected match for export { foo } from");
}
```

### Investigation findings

1. **Mapping exists**: `JsExportNamedFromClause` is correctly mapped in `generated_mappings.rs`.

2. **Snippet parsing issue**: The pattern `export { $foo } from $source` gets incorrectly parsed as `JS_OBJECT_MEMBER_LIST` or `JSX_CHILD_LIST` instead of `JS_EXPORT_NAMED_FROM_CLAUSE`.

3. **Likely cause**: The `snippet_context_strings()` in `crates/biome_grit_patterns/src/grit_target_language/js_target_language.rs` may not provide adequate context for parsing `export { ... } from ...` statements.


### Expected result

The GritQL pattern `export { $foo } from $source` should match the code `export { foo } from "../../deep/path";` and emit a diagnostic, just like `export * from $source` does for `export * from "../../deep/path";`.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#8828.title: 🐛 GritQL pattern `export { $foo } from $source` fails to match while `export * from $source` works

### What happened?

Building a hidey-bar sticky navigation based on [Una Kravet's Demo](https://una.im/scroll-state-scrolled).

The Demo combines `not` and `scroll-state` in a container query which leads the parser to fail.
I've added 3 different combinations in [playground](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%3D&language=css) that all seem to work in Chrome (only browser with `scroll-state` support). 

The first example is based on Una's Demo, the second example (no parenthesis) is based on the [MDN Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At-rules/@container#scrollable) and the third one uses yet another grouping (fails with a different error).

### Expected result

Parser should not fail

### Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#8882.body:
### Environment information I am developing in Antigravity on Windows 11.
```bash CLI: Version: 2.3.12 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: x86_64 OS: windows Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset BIOME_THREADS: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: unset JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: v24.11.1 JS_RUNTIME_NAME: node NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: unset Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Path: biome.jsonc Formatter enabled: true Linter enabled: true Assist enabled: true VCS enabled: true HTML full support enabled: true Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ```
### What happened? Issue I came across trying out biome in an astro project related to multi-line comments in the astro frontmatter. If you have a multi line comment like the below, that contains something that could be a string opener (without a closer to match) then the rest of the file will show a bunch of errors: ```astro --- /** * In this comment, if you add any string opening or closing, such as an apostrophy, the file will show * a bunch of errors. Doesn't (remove the apostrophy in the previous word to fix) that stink? */ import type { HTMLAttributes } from "astro/types"; type Props = HTMLAttributes<"div">; const { class: className, ...rest } = Astro.props; ---
``` [playground demo](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%%2BAAoAIAAgADwAZABpAHYAIABjAGwAYQBzAHMAPQAiAHAAdAAtADAAIABwAGIALQA0ACIAPgAKACAAIAAgACAAPABzAGwAbwB0ACAALwA%2BAAoAIAAgADwALwBkAGkAdgA%2BAAoAPAAvAGQAaQB2AD4ACgA%3D&language=astro) ### Expected result This should not cause errors, as anything in a comment should be valid and not interfere with later parsing. ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#8897.body:
### Environment information

A forward slash within a `@utility` tailwind directive name throws an error when parsing it, as in the repro

Prettier is able to parse this kind of css, while biome throws an ```expected `{` but instead found `/` ``` error

While the css is not valid, tailwind automatically escapes it, incidentally there is also an issue where tailwind doesn't allow the pre-escaped utility name which would work with biome. Still, maybe this is worth a fix on both sides

```BASH
  > 79 │     animation-delay: calc(sibling-index() * 100ms);
       │                           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^

    81 │ 
  
  ℹ Use a known function instead.

```

lint/correctness/noUnknownFunction

sibling-index and sibling-count is a real thing on CSS.

Maybe we can flag as a soft warning because dont have 100% Browser compatibility? I don't know. Don't think that this is useful but...

biomejs/biome#9013.body:
### Environment information
```bash CLI: Version: 0.0.0 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: x86_64 OS: linux Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_LOG_LEVEL: unset BIOME_LOG_KIND: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset BIOME_THREADS: unset BIOME_WATCHER_KIND: unset BIOME_WATCHER_POLLING_INTERVAL: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: xterm-256color JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: unset JS_RUNTIME_NAME: unset NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: unset Biome Configuration: Error: The configuration schema version does not match the CLI version 0.0.0 Status: Loaded with errors Path: biome.json Formatter enabled: false Linter enabled: true Assist enabled: false VCS enabled: true HTML full support enabled: true Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ```
### What happened? This is a bug to be fixed on `next`. https://next.biomejs.dev/playground/?code=PABzAGMAcgBpAHAAdAAgAHMAZQB0AHUAcAA%%2BAAoACgA8AGIAdQB0AHQAbwBuAAoAIAAgAHQAeQBwAGUAPQAiAGIAdQB0AHQAbwBuACIACgAgACAAOgBkAGkAcwBhAGIAbABlAGQAPQAiACEAcwB1AHAAcABvAHIAdABlAGQAIgAKACAAIABAAGMAbABpAGMAawA9ACIAdABvAGcAZwBsAGUAQwBhAHAAdABpAG8AbgBzACgAKQAiAAoAPgAKACAAIABjAGwAaQBjAGsACgA8AC8AYgB1AHQAdABvAG4APgA%3D&language=vue&ruleDomains.react=recommended Currently, expressions in Vue v-on directives (both longhand `v-on:` and shorthand `@`) don't mark any of the variables they use as used. In Vue, the value of a v-on directive is parsed as the body of an arrow function with no `{}` braces around it. My IDE decorates it like this: ### Expected result `noUnusedVariables` should not trigger here. ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9013.title: 🐛 Vue `v-on` directives (and shorthand) false positive with `noUnusedVariables` on `next`

biomejs/biome#9020.body:
### Environment information
```bash CLI: Version: 2.3.14 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: x86_64 OS: linux Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset BIOME_THREADS: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: xterm-256color JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: v25.2.1 JS_RUNTIME_NAME: node NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: unset Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Path: biome.jsonc Formatter enabled: true Linter enabled: true Assist enabled: true VCS enabled: true HTML full support enabled: unset Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ```
### What happened? [is_global_react_import](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/9d4cfa304268579361639c3d2301f1e38526dd00/crates/biome_js_analyze/src/react.rs#L305) is used by `lint/correctness/noUnusedImports` and `lint/style/useImportType` to not accidentally mark the React global as unused, as the classic React JSX transform requires it in scope. However, the function unnecessarily checks the import name against [`ReactLibrary::import_names`](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/9d4cfa304268579361639c3d2301f1e38526dd00/crates/biome_js_analyze/src/react.rs#L122) to verify that it's actually React, and this behavior doesn't align with the rest of the ecosystem ([TypeScript](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/blob/cdb583a81357553fd72faedc6602c3f2516490fc/src/services/organizeImports.ts#L365-L369 ) simply accepts any import with the identifier of "React"). A project of mine [re-exports React](https://github.com/moonlight-mod/moonlight/blob/becf5cb3d182bf2f1d68700bbe29e5edb0a04035/packages/mappings/src/mappings/react.ts#L3-L6), so `is_global_react_import` returns false, and these lints don't work properly as a result: ```tsx import React from "@moonlight-mod/wp/react"; export function Test() { return Hello, world!; } ``` ``` packages/core-extensions/src/test.tsx:1:8 lint/correctness/noUnusedImports FIXABLE ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ⚠ This import is unused. > 1 │ import React from "@moonlight-mod/wp/react"; │ ^^^^^ 2 │ 3 │ export function Test() { ℹ Unused imports might be the result of an incomplete refactoring. ℹ Unsafe fix: Remove the unused imports. 1 │ import·React·from·"@moonlight-mod/wp/react"; │ -------------------------------------------- Checked 1 file in 7ms. No fixes applied. Found 1 warning. ``` If I use React as a type (e.g. set the return type to `React.ReactNode`), `useImportType` kicks in. This is arguably worse, as it contains a "safe fix" that prevents the code from compiling: ``` $ biome check packages/core-extensions/src/test.tsx:1:8 lint/style/useImportType FIXABLE ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ⚠ All these imports are only used as types. > 1 │ import React from "@moonlight-mod/wp/react"; │ ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 2 │ 3 │ export function Test(): React.ReactNode { ℹ Importing the types with import type ensures that they are removed by the compilers and avoids loading unnecessary modules. ℹ Safe fix: Use import type. 1 │ import·type·React·from·"@moonlight-mod/wp/react"; │ +++++ Checked 1 file in 10ms. No fixes applied. Found 1 warning. ``` ``` $ tsc --noEmit src/test.tsx:4:11 - error TS1361: 'React' cannot be used as a value because it was imported using 'import type'. 4 return Hello, world!; ~~~~ src/test.tsx:1:8 1 import type React from "@moonlight-mod/wp/react"; ~~~~~~~~~~ 'React' was imported here. Found 1 error in src/test.tsx:4 ``` ### Expected result `is_global_react_import` shouldn't enforce the import name, to match behavior with the rest of the ecosystem. This is also how esbuild and [@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/no-unused-vars/) functions, but I'm not entirely sure what the responsible code is in those projects. Alternatively, some way to configure or disable this behavior would be nice. Judging by https://github.com/biomejs/biome/pull/7488, this also seems to have caused problems for [@rbxts/react](https://github.com/littensy/rbxts-react) in the past, however that PR simply added the package name to the hardcoded list. "I do not foresee a significant amount of users otherwise ever having to modify this behavior to point to a separate package"... oops. ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9020.title: 🐛 React import checks are stricter than they should be

### Rule name

lint/a11y/noInteractiveElementToNoninteractiveRole

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%2BAA%3D%3D

### Expected result

The `hr` tag follows the allowed aria-roles for that tag: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/hr#technical_summary

You can also see that "presentation" and "none" should be allowed: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Roles/presentation_role#associated_wai-aria_roles_states_and_properties

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#9052.body:
I'm not good at English, so I use a translator 🙏

### Environment information

<details>

```bash
CLI:
  Version:                      2.3.15
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             x86_64
  OS:                           linux

Environment:

  BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME:        unset

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Path:                         biome.json
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Linter enabled:               true
  Assist enabled:               true
  VCS enabled:                  false
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

</details>


### What happened?

1. Create monorepo
2. Specify `"//"` for extends and `!directory` in `files.includes` (ex. `"!world"`)
3. Directories that specify to ignore are not ignored.

Please see this repository for minimal reproduce codes.
https://github.com/kazuemon/biomejs-monorepo-ignore-reproduction

### Expected result

Works `!directory` in `files.includes` even if specify `"//"` for extends (specified directory should be ignore)

FYI: In minimal reproduce codes, `!packages/fuga/world` is worked, but `!world` not worked.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9052.title: 🐛 Ignore syntax doesn't work only when using microsyntax in extends

### What happened?

https://next.biomejs.dev/playground/?analyzerFixMode=applySuppressions&enabledAssist=false&%3D%3D&language=vue

See the Analyzer Fixes tab. It added a suppression comment when it should not.

Note that no diagnostic gets emitted.  So there's something specifically on the `--suppress` code path that is causing the suppression comment to erroneously get emitted.

### Rule name

noProcessEnv

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?indentWidth=4&lintRules=noProcessEnv&%3D&language=ts

### Expected result

The lint should also trigger when **importing** the `process` object or the `env` object inside it, not just when using the global `process` object.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9061.title: 💅 `noProcessEnv` only works with the global `process` object (false negative)

biomejs/biome#9062.body:
### Description

The rule would disallow the use of inline styles, see https://html-eslint.org/docs/rules/no-inline-styles

In addition to the reasons listed under the URL above, inline styles are also problematic when wanting to set up a strict Content Security Policy. 
See https://content-security-policy.com/examples/allow-inline-style for more info.
biomejs/biome#9062.title: 📎 Port `no-inline-styles` from `html-eslint`

### Expected result

After updating to `2.4.0`, I am getting false positives with the `noUnknownPseudoElement` lint rule on very standard ones like `::after`. After playing in the playground above, it appears only when I activate CSS Modules (which in my repo, I am). When I deactivate CSS Modules, the error disappears.

```bash
  Linter enabled:               true

  VCS enabled:                  false
  HTML full support enabled:    unset

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0


```

</details>


### What happened?

It seems like biome 2.4 fails to parse SCSS interpolation correctly which is necessary for e.g. assigning a scss var to a css var.

Here is an example:
1. Put the following code into a "index.scss" file. (couldnt put it into the playground because it doesnt support scss?)
```
$red: #a5031e;
:root {
        --bs-primary: #{$red};
}
```

2. Run `biome check` on the file. 

Output: 
```
  ✖ Expected a qualified rule, or an at rule but instead found ';
    }'.
  
    1 │ $red: #a5031e;
    2 │ :root {
  > 3 │ 	--bs-primary: #{$red};
      │ 	                    ^
  > 4 │ }
      │ ^
    5 │ 
  
  ℹ Expected a qualified rule, or an at rule here.
  
    1 │ $red: #a5031e;
    2 │ :root {
  > 3 │ 	--bs-primary: #{$red};
      │ 	                    ^
  > 4 │ }
      │ ^
    5 │ 
```
 

### Expected result

The file should be accepted and processed like in biome 2.3

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9090.title: 🐛 2.4 fails to parse scss interpolation correctly

```typescript
export const test = {a: 1}
```

In Svelte, when I import an enum as value and use it as a value only in the HTML body, I'd expect the import to be left alone.

> [!NOTE]
> I understand that your docs say that we shouldn't expect JS-in-____ to work yet with `experimentalFullSupportEnabled`, but this is one of very few issues blocking my org from adopting Biome.

biomejs/biome#9109.body:
### Description

[The docs on the `ci` command](https://biomejs.dev/recipes/continuous-integration/#biome-check-vs-biome-ci) claim:
> Compared to the `check` command, the `ci` command:
> * ... when run on GitHub, the diagnostics are printed using the GitHub annotations.

However, this behavior is not currently implemented.

Ideally, the equivalent to the options `--reporter=default --reporter=github` should be enabled when `biome ci` is run within GitHub Actions. The default reporter should be kept for human readability.

---
The `ci` command internals do have the infrastructure to [detect when run in a GitHub Actions environment](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/4a5ff4034d8406d7de28aa6ce8095987c3fef547/crates/biome_cli/src/commands/ci.rs#L147-L149), but nothing is apparently done with the information (nothing appears to reference `CiExecution._environment` nor change behavior when it is `ExecutionEnvironment::GitHub`).
biomejs/biome#9109.title: 📎 `ci` command should automatically enable `github` reporter when appropriate

biomejs/biome#9115.body:
### Environment information

## Description

The `nursery/noPlaywrightMissingAwait` rule produces **false positives** on projects that use **React Testing Library** with **jest-dom** (or Vitest with `@testing-library/jest-dom`).

The root cause is that `ASYNC_PLAYWRIGHT_MATCHERS` (the internal list the rule uses to identify Playwright async matchers) contains matcher names that **also exist in jest-dom as synchronous matchers**. The rule only checks the matcher name on an `expect()` call — it does not distinguish between Playwright's async `expect` and Vitest/Jest's synchronous `expect`.

### Overlapping matchers (12 total)

The following matchers appear in **both** `ASYNC_PLAYWRIGHT_MATCHERS` and `@testing-library/jest-dom`:

| Matcher | Playwright (async) | jest-dom (sync) |
|---|---|---|

### What happened?

## How to reproduce

A minimal reproduction repository is available at: **https://github.com/THernandez03/biome-noPlaywrightMissingAwait**

### Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/THernandez03/biome-noPlaywrightMissingAwait.git
cd biome-noPlaywrightMissingAwait
npm install
npm run check
```

## Additional context

- The `noPlaywrightMissingAwait` rule was introduced in Biome **2.4.2** (nursery).
- The overlap affects any project that uses **both** Playwright (e2e tests) and jest-dom (unit/integration tests) in the same repository, which is a very common setup.
- The jest-dom matchers listed above are all **synchronous** — they return `void`, not a `Promise`. Adding `await` to them is unnecessary and misleading.


### Expected result

The rule should **only flag** matchers when the `expect` originates from `@playwright/test`. When `expect` is imported from `vitest`, `jest`, or any non-Playwright source, the rule should **not** fire — even if the matcher name matches one in `ASYNC_PLAYWRIGHT_MATCHERS`.

Possible approaches:
1. **Check the import source** of `expect` — only flag when it comes from `@playwright/test`.
2. **Check the file path** — skip files outside the Playwright test directory (e.g., not matching the Playwright `testDir` pattern).
3. **Respect domain scoping** — if a file doesn't belong to the `playwright` domain, don't apply the rule to it.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Environment information

Minimal repro at [https://github.com/petrzelka/biome-repro-1771308898252](https://github.com/petrzelka/biome-repro-1771308898252)

Svelte file 
```
<script lang="ts">
	const line1 = "fail";
	console.log(line1);
</script>
```
is not changed, only updated timestamp. Issue happens only with `"indentScriptAndStyle": true`

### What happened?

`npx biome check` 
`Checked 3 files in 3ms. No fixes applied.`

`npx biome check --write` 
`Checked 3 files in 27ms. Fixed 1 file.`

`npx biome check --write` 
`Checked 3 files in 27ms. Fixed 1 file.`

### Expected result

Should not touch files that are not changed

biomejs/biome#9141.body:
### Environment information

#### Description

When enabling the `noUnknownAttribute` rule, **Biome** reports the `closedby` attribute on the `<dialog>` element as an unknown attribute.

However, `closedby` is a valid HTML attribute for `<dialog>`, as documented in the MDN reference:  
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/dialog

This results in false positives, warning users about attributes that are actually part of the HTML specification.

### Current Behavior

The linter flags `closedby` as an unknown attribute when used on a `<dialog>` element.

[See the playground](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lineWidth=100&indentStyle=space&quoteStyle=single&lintRules=noUnknownAttribute&cssModules=true&code=PABkAGkAYQBsAG8AZwAgAG4AYQBtAGUAPQAiAHYAYQBsAGkAZAAiACAAYwBsAG8AcwBlAGQAYgB5AD0AIgBhAG4AeQAiACAAdQBuAGsAbgBvAHcAbgA9ACIAdAByAHUAZQAiAC8APgAKAA%3D%3D&ruleDomains.react=all&ruleDomains.project=all&ruleDomains.test=all&ruleDomains.solid=all&ruleDomains.next=all)

This triggers a `noUnknownAttribute` warning.

### Expected Behavior

The `noUnknownAttribute` rule should:

- Warn only about truly unknown or invalid attributes
- Accept valid attributes defined for each HTML element, including `closedby` on `<dialog>`

### Possible solution

By inspecting the implementation, the issue appears to come from a missing entry in the attribute map.

In this file:
https://github.com/biomejs/biome/blob/792013eb767188608d0b6dd4dfa24413832dcb93/crates/biome_js_analyze/src/lint/nursery/no_unknown_attribute.rs#L103

The variable `ATTRIBUTE_TAGS_MAP` contains the list of valid attributes for each HTML element. The `<dialog>` entry does not currently include the `closedby` attribute.

Adding `closedby` to the list of allowed attributes for the `dialog` element in `ATTRIBUTE_TAGS_MAP` should resolve the issue and prevent false positives.

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/dialog#closedby

### What happened?

Starting from Biome `2.4.0`, the parser and linter are producing false positives within Vue SFC templates:

1. `parse` **error**: Biome incorrectly attempts to parse attribute values containing spaces (UnoCSS/TailwindCSS class groupings) as single JavaScript expressions – `Template expressions can only contain a single expression`.

2. `noAssignInExpressions` **error**: Biome triggers a lint error for assignments within `@click` handlers, which are standard and valid in Vue template syntax – `The assignment should not be in an expression`.

This worked as expected in `2.3.15` and earlier Biome versions.

### Actual Behavior

<details>

<summary>Example Biome Output</summary>

```
components\Counter.vue:31:26 parse ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  × Template expressions can only contain a single expression

    29 │       font="mono"
    30 │       outline="!none"
  > 31 │       hover:bg="gray-400 opacity-20"
       │                          ^^^^^^^
    32 │       @click="counter += 1"
    33 │     >

  i Remove the extra code or split into multiple template expressions.


components\Counter.vue:32:15 lint/suspicious/noAssignInExpressions ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  × The assignment should not be in an expression.

    30 │       outline="!none"
    31 │       hover:bg="gray-400 opacity-20"
  > 32 │       @click="counter += 1"
       │               ^^^^^^^^^^^^
    33 │     >
    34 │       +

  i The use of assignments in expressions is confusing.
    Expressions are often considered as side-effect free.
```

</details>

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a `.vue` file with the following content:

```
<template>
  <button
    type = "button"
    hover:bg="gray-400 opacity-20"
    @click="counter += 1"
  >
    +
  </button>
</template>
```

2. Run `biome check` or `biome lint` using version `2.4.0` or later.

### Minimal Reproduction

[Playground Link](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%3D&language=vue)

### Expected result

There should be no errors.

1. `hover:bg="gray-400 opacity-20"` should be treated as a static attribute string, not a template expression.
2. `@click="counter += 1"` should be recognized as a valid Vue event handler assignment and should not trigger `noAssignInExpressions`.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#9174.body:
### Environment information

N/A

### Rule name

`lint/nursery/useExpect`

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lineWidth=120&indentStyle=space&lintRules=useExpect&analyzerFixMode=safeAndUnsafeFixes&%3D

### Expected result

This should produce an error - `expect.stringContaining` is a Vitest asymmetric matcher whose only purpose is to be passed to an actual `expect` call (and is not an assertion itself).
As for `expect.extend`, it augments Vitest's `extend` syntax with the provided function, and is similarly not actually testing anything.

A full list of Vitest constructs that shouldn't be counted as assertions:
- Built-in asymmetric matchers (`expect.stringContaining`, `expect.objectContaining`, `expect.anything`, `expect.any`,`expect.closeTo`,`expect.arrayContaining`, `expect.stringMatching`, `expect.schemaMatching`)
- `expect` helper funcs (`expect.addSnapshotSerializer`, `expect.addEqualityTesters`, `expect.extend`, `expect.hasAssertions`)

#### Issues

From what I can tell, this is made a lot harder since [`expect.extend`](https://vitest.dev/api/expect.html#expect-extend) will add both asymmetric matchers AND custom assertion functions with the same name.

```ts
it("a", () => {
  expect.extend({myFooMatcher});

  // these are both valid syntaxes
  expect(new Foo()).toBe(expect.myFooMatcher());
  expect(new Foo()).myFooMatcher();
});
```

If nothing else, we should mention this limitation in the rule's docs, or provide a way to specify what types of `expect.XYZ` syntaxes are/aren't valid assertions. 

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9174.title: 💅 `useExpect` produces false negatives for Vitest `expect.XYZ` functions that are not valid assertions

When opening the issue, please provide a minimal reproduction, or identify and share the file/code that triggers it. biomejs/biome#9189.body:
### What happened?

Following the change in #9120, running `biome ci` now enables the `github` reporter when the environment contains `GITHUB_ACTION=true`. However, by default, `biome ci` still uses color output, which currently breaks the intended functionality.

Specifically, Biome's color output causes lines of `github` reporter output (written to stdout) to be wrapped with the characters `0x1b 0x5b 0x30 0x6d` (or `ESC[0m`), which is the [ANSI escape sequence](https://gist.github.com/ConnerWill/d4b6c776b509add763e17f9f113fd25b#colors--graphics-mode) to "reset all modes (styles and colors)". These extra characters breaks GitHub's ability to process such lines [as workflow commands](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-commands#setting-an-error-message), which defeats the purpose of the `github` reporter.

Note, if the command is run as `biome ci --reporter=default --reporter=github` color output is disabled (due to #3281) and the output is correctly detected by GitHub. However, `biome ci` ought to "just work" too.

---
I think there are 2 potential fixes:
1. Make `biome ci` (when `GITHUB_ACTION=true`) actually work the same as `--reporter=default --reporter=github`, which would effectively also set `--colors=off`, per #3281.
2. Ensure that the stdout output of the `github` has "clean" lines with no ANSI control characters. I suspect that control characters could still technically be output by the `github` reporter (if colors needed to be reset), but they'd have to be on totally separate lines from the actual "workflow commands". Additionally, maybe part of #3281 could actually be reverted, so that when `--reporter=default --reporter=github` is explicitly enabled, the `default` reporter could still produce color output.

I think 2 would be the most user-friendly behavior. However, 1 seems easier and would only deprive users of colors when running inside GitHub Actions.


### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9189.title: 🐛 GitHub reporter output is invalid when colors are enabled

biomejs/biome#9230.body:
### Description

Follow-up to #8043 / PR #8952.

Extend `useNullishCoalescing` to detect `||=` assignment expressions where the left-hand side is possibly nullish and suggest rewriting them as `??=`.

**Pattern to detect:**

- `a ||= b` -> `a ??= b`

The existing type-eligibility and safety logic from the `||` -> `??` check applies directly here. The main work is adding a new query target for `JsAssignmentExpression` with the `||=` operator, reusing `is_possibly_nullish` and `is_safe_type_for_replacement` to gate the diagnostic and fix.

The fix itself is a straightforward token replacement of `||=` with `??=`, same approach as the existing `||` -> `??` fix.

**References:**

- ESLint rule: `AssignmentExpression[operator = "||="]` visitor in [prefer-nullish-coalescing](https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/blob/main/packages/eslint-plugin/src/rules/prefer-nullish-coalescing.ts)
biomejs/biome#9230.title: 📎 `useNullishCoalescing`: suggest `??=` for logical OR assignment (`||=`)
