biomejs/biome#9245.body:
### Environment information
```bash × The elements with this role can be changed to the following elements: 23 │ aria-live="polite" 24 │ className="container mx-auto max-w-3xl px-4 py-6" > 25 │ role="status" │ ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 26 │ > 27 │ Loading dashboard i For examples and more information, see WAI-ARIA Roles Checked 1 file in 6ms. No fixes applied. Found 1 error. check ```
### Environment @biomejs/biome: 2.4.4 React + TSX ### What happened? `lint/a11y/useSemanticElements` currently suggests using `` instead of a `
` for status messages such as loading indicators. This conflicts with ARIA guidance to: - Use native HTML elements when they match the needed semantics, but reserve `` for calculation results / form-related output, not generic status messages. - A generic “Loading dashboard…” indicator is a status message and is not the result of a calculation or form interaction. - ARIA’s own guidance is “prefer native semantics when they fit; otherwise use roles”, not “replace all role="status" with ``”. In other words, `` is not the correct replacement for every role="status". Here is a similar discussion on [sonarsource](https://community.sonarsource.com/t/typescript-rspec-6819-should-not-always-require-htmls-output-element-instead-of-aria-status-role/133611l) ### Minimal repro ```tsx
Loading dashboard
``` ### Expected result Should not suggest `` for a generic status message like a loading indicator. The rule should either: - Only suggest `` when the element clearly represents the result of a calculation or form output, or - Provide a configuration option to disable this specific suggestion. ### Suggested change Adjust the rule (likely useSemanticElements or related a11y rule) so that: - It does not recommend as a blanket replacement for role="status". - It considers context (e.g., form association, for/name attributes, calculator-like UI) before recommending ``. Alternatively, downgrade this to a weaker recommendation or allow opting out via rule configuration. ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9245.title: lint/a11y: Prefer native element rule incorrectly suggests <output> instead of role="status" for loading indicators

biomejs/biome#9249.title: 📝 forward slash with comment breaks rule

biomejs/biome#9250.body:
### Environment information vue linter rule `noVueDuplicateKeys` is throwing a false positive on prop that is used on watch.
Minimal replication vue component: ``` ``` Rule output: ``` /main.vue:11:4 [lint/correctness/noVueDuplicateKeys](https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-vue-duplicate-keys) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ✖ Duplicate key formData found in Vue component. 9 │ modelValue: [String, Number, Object], 10 │ > 11 │ formData: { │ ^^^^^^^^ 12 │ type: Object, 13 │ default: () => ({}), ℹ Key formData is also defined here. 18 │ watch: { > 19 │ formData: { │ ^^^^^^^^ 20 │ handler(newValue) 21 │ { ```
### Rule name noVueDuplicateKeys ### Playground link https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lintRules=noVueDuplicateKeys&code=PAB0AGUAbQBwAGwAYQB0AGUAPgAKACAAIAA8AGQAaQB2AD4AeAA8AC8AZABpAHYAPgAKADwALwB0AGUAbQBwAGwAYQB0AGUAPgAKAAoAPABzAGMAcgBpAHAAdAA%%3D%3D&language=vue ### Expected result This is a necessary and correct vue component definition. The rule is too wide reaching, it should not lookup duplicate keys in different sections of the component. ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9250.title: 💅 False positive for vue noVueDuplicateKeys

biomejs/biome#9258.body:
## Summary

When running `biome check --skip=project`, suppressions that target project-domain diagnostics can become `suppressions/unused` warnings and fail CI with `--error-on-warnings`.

This seems surprising because skipping a domain is intended to exclude that domain's checks, but it introduces a new warning from another rule.

## Biome version

`2.4.3`

## Platform

- macOS 26.4 (arm64)

## Minimal repro

### 1) Create files

`package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "biome-repro",
  "private": true
}
```

`biome.jsonc`

```jsonc
{
  "linter": {
    "domains": {
      "project": "recommended"
    },
    "rules": {
      "correctness": {
        "noUndeclaredDependencies": "on"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

`index.ts`

```ts
// biome-ignore lint/correctness/noUndeclaredDependencies: repro for --skip=project interaction
import { foo } from "not-installed-package";

void foo;
```

### 2) Run without skipping project domain

```bash
bunx biome check index.ts --error-on-warnings
```

Result: exits `0`

### 3) Run with `--skip=project`

```bash
bunx biome check index.ts --error-on-warnings --skip=project
```

Result: exits `1` with:

- `suppressions/unused` at line 1

## Expected behavior

If `project` diagnostics are skipped, suppressions for project-domain diagnostics should not produce a new `suppressions/unused` warning (or there should be a way to keep this from failing in this mode).

## Actual behavior

`--skip=project` causes:

- `suppressions/unused` warning
- non-zero exit when `--error-on-warnings` is enabled

This makes `--skip=project` difficult to use in existing codebases that have valid suppressions for project rules.
biomejs/biome#9258.title: --skip=project triggers suppressions/unused for project-rule suppressions

biomejs/biome#9263.body:
## Context

PR #9255 fixed a panic (reported in #9234) that occurred when lint rules declared their options type as `type Options = ()` instead of using the generated options struct (e.g., `NoHeaderScopeOptions`).

## Problem

Currently, the rules check harness does not validate that lint rules use proper options types. When rules use `type Options = ()`, it can lead to `Option::unwrap()` panics in `biome_analyze/src/options.rs`.

## Proposed Solution

Enhance the rules check harness to forbid lint rules from declaring `type Options = ()`. All rules should use their generated options types (e.g., `RuleNameOptions`), even if the struct is empty.

## References

- Fixed in: #9255
- Original panic report: #9234
biomejs/biome#9263.title: Improve rules check harness to forbid `type Options = ()`

```json
        "linter": {
                "enabled": true,
                "rules": {
                        "recommended": true,
                        "style": "error"
                }
        }
}

```

and the following index.js

```js
const x = "hello world";
const y = x.substring(0);
console.log(y);


```

the result should include the 'noSubstr' error... however it does not, the result of a 'check' are as follows:

```bash
% npx @biomejs/biome check        
Checked 3 files in 20ms. No fixes applied.
```

the above playground link produces the same issue.

you can generate the noSubstr error with code `"hello world".substring(0)` directly (so the rule does "work" in some cases)

> Biome encountered an unexpected error
> 
> This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue.
> 
> When opening the issue, please provide a minimal reproduction, or identify and share the file/code that triggers it. Without a way to reproduce the error, the error can't be fixed:
> 
> Source Location: /Users/runner/work/biome/biome/crates/biome_analyze/src/options.rs:27:35
> Thread Name: biome::workspace_worker_0

It should not fail.

biomejs/biome#9341.body:
### Environment information

```bash

CLI:
  Version:                      2.4.5
  Color support:                true

Platform:
  CPU Architecture:             x86_64
  OS:                           windows

Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Loaded successfully
  Formatter enabled:            true
  Assist enabled:               true

Formatter:
  Format with errors:           true
  Indent style:                 Tab
  Line width:                   90
  Includes:                     **/*.ts, **/*.tsx

```

### Description

When saving a TypeScript file, the Biome LSP server crashes **and actively corrupts the file content** — replacing existing code with garbage text.

**Error output from VS Code Output panel:**
```
Caused by:
    position Position { line: 445, character: 0 } is out of range
2026-03-05 10:21:31.846 [info] Biome encountered an unexpected error

This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue.

Source Location: crates\biome_lsp\src\utils.rs:405:22
Thread Name: tokio-runtime-worker
Message: assertion failed: self.is_char_boundary(n)
Stack Trace: Re-run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` to capture the stack trace
```

**Actual file corruption observed (git diff):**
```diff
-               messages,
-               childMessages,
-               queries,
-               // callbacks
+
+
+
+
+
+
+               a
+
+               sendInputMes
```


### Steps to reproduce

1. Open any TypeScript file with Biome as default formatter and `formatOnSave: true`
2. Make any edit (e.g. add a newline)
3. Save (`Ctrl+S`)
4. LSP crashes and file content is corrupted


### Configuration

```JSON
{
  "formatter": {
    "enabled": true,
    "lineWidth": 90,
    "formatWithErrors": true
  },
  "assist": {
    "actions": {
      "source": {
        "organizeImports": { "level": "on" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Playground link

https://github.com/JanSotek/biome-repro-1772777797596

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#9349.body:
### Environment information
```bash $ biome rage CLI: Version: 2.4.4 Color support: true Platform: CPU Architecture: aarch64 OS: macos Environment: BIOME_LOG_PATH: unset BIOME_LOG_PREFIX_NAME: unset BIOME_LOG_LEVEL: unset BIOME_LOG_KIND: unset BIOME_CONFIG_PATH: unset BIOME_THREADS: unset BIOME_WATCHER_KIND: unset BIOME_WATCHER_POLLING_INTERVAL: unset NO_COLOR: unset TERM: xterm-256color JS_RUNTIME_VERSION: v25.7.0 JS_RUNTIME_NAME: node NODE_PACKAGE_MANAGER: bun/1.3.10 Biome Configuration: Status: Loaded successfully Path: biome.json Formatter enabled: true Linter enabled: true Assist enabled: true VCS enabled: true HTML full support enabled: true Workspace: Open Documents: 0 ```
### What happened? `useAltText` triggers a false positive in Vue SFCs when the `alt` attribute is bound dynamically via `:alt` (i.e. `v-bind:alt`). ```vue ``` Playground reproduction: [Playground](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?code=PAB0AGUAbQBwAGwAYQB0AGUAPgAKACAAIAA8AGkAbQBnACAAOgBzAHIAYwA9ACIAaQBtAGEAZwBlAC4AcwByAGMAIgAgADoAYQBsAHQAPQAiAGkAbQBhAGcAZQAuAGQAZQBzAGMAcgBpAHAAdABpAG8AbgAiACAALwA%2BAAoAPAAvAHQAZQBtAHAAbABhAHQAZQA%2BAA%3D%3D&language=vue) ### Expected result `:alt="..."` should be treated as a valid `alt` attribute, equivalent to `alt="..."`, and should suppress the `useAltText` diagnostic. ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9349.title: 🐛 lint/a11y/useAltText: False positive for Vue dynamic :alt binding in .vue files

### Environment information

This is a flaky error, I'm not sure about the rate but reruns pass. I am encountering this error in CI checking about 7159 files with nested configs. If it's a misconfiguration on my end, there's not enough info to figure out what's wrong. So, this issue is a request to include more details about what's going wrong in the cli so I can act upon it.

### Rule name

lint/style/useImportType

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?indentScriptAndStyle=true&files.main.%2BAFsAMABdACAAPQAgACIAZABlAGYAYQB1AGwAdAAiADsACgA8AC8AcwBjAHIAaQBwAHQAPgAKAAoAPABiAHUAdAB0AG8AbgAgAHQAeQBwAGUAPQAiAGIAdQB0AHQAbwBuACIAIABjAGwAYQBzAHMAPQB7AGIAdQB0AHQAbwBuAEMAbABhAHMAcwAoAHYAYQByAGkAYQBuAHQAKQB9AD4AQgB1AHQAdABvAG4APAAvAGIAdQB0AHQAbwBuAD4ACgA%3D&files.buttonClass.%2FACAAIgBiAGcALQBnAHIAYQB5AC0AMgAwADAAIAB0AGUAeAB0AC0AYgBsAGEAYwBrACIACgAJAAkACQA6ACAAIgBiAGcALQBiAGwAdQBlAC0ANQAwADAAIAB0AGUAeAB0AC0AdwBoAGkAdABlACIAOwAKAAkAcgBlAHQAdQByAG4AIABgACQAewBiAGEAcwBlAH0AIAAkAHsAdgBhAHIAaQBhAG4AdABDAGwAYQBzAHMAfQBgADsACgB9AAoA

### Expected result

**Steps to Reproduce**

1. Create a runtime function buttonClass.ts:
```ts
// buttonClass.ts
export function buttonClass(variant: "default" | "secondary" = "default") {
  return variant === "secondary" ? "bg-gray-200" : "bg-blue-500";
}
```
2. Create a Svelte component Button.svelte:
```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import { buttonClass } from "./buttonClass.ts";

  export let variant: Parameters<typeof buttonClass>[0] = "default";
</script>

<button class={buttonClass(variant)}>Test</button>
```

**Expected Result**

Biome should not report any warning because buttonClass is actually used at runtime in the template.

The component is valid TypeScript and Svelte 5.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

```bash
Biome Configuration:
  Status:                       Failed to load
  Error:                        Failed to resolve the configuration from @kcconfigs/biome/features/svelte

Workspace:
  Open Documents:               0
```

</details>


### What happened?

> I created reproduce repository here: https://github.com/kamontat/biome-issue-9370

When publish package when more than 2 slash, 
the module resolve seem to not able to resolve the package even the path is set up correctly.

```bash
  ✖ Failed to resolve the configuration from @kcconfigs/biome/features/svelte
    
    Caused by:
      Could not resolve @kcconfigs/biome/features/svelte: module not found
```

### Expected result

It should not throw any error

### Rule name

lint/style/useImportType

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?%%2BAAoACQAJADwAZABpAHYAIABjAGwAYQBzAHMAPQAiAGYAbABlAHgAIABpAHQAZQBtAHMALQBjAGUAbgB0AGUAcgAgAGcAYQBwAC0AMgAiAD4ACgAJAAkACQA8AHMAcABhAG4APgB7AHQAZQB4AHQAfQA8AC8AcwBwAGEAbgA%2BAAoACQAJAAkAewBiAGEAZABnAGUAIAAmACYAIAA8AEIAYQBkAGcAZQAgAHQAZQB4AHQAPQB7AGIAYQBkAGcAZQAuAHQAZQB4AHQAfQAgAHYAYQByAGkAYQBuAHQAPQB7AGIAYQBkAGcAZQAuAHYAYQByAGkAYQBuAHQAfQAgAC8APgB9AAoACQAJADwALwBkAGkAdgA%2BAAoACQA8AC8AYQA%2BAAoAPAAvAGwAaQA%2BAAoA&language=astro

### Expected result

No `useImportType` warning, because the import is used at runtime in the Astro template.

I tested this pattern, and what happens is:

- `import Badge ...` + runtime usage in template (`<Badge ... />`) without `ComponentProps<typeof Badge>` results in no warning
- Add `badge?: ComponentProps<typeof Badge>` results in Biome warning `useImportType`, even when `<Badge />` is still used at runtime

So this looks like a Biome false positive caused by the `ComponentProps<typeof Badge>` usage pattern

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9432.title: 💅 False positive `useImportType` in `.astro` when component import is used in template and in `ComponentProps<typeof ...>`

### What happened?

When I get "Biome found a configuration file outside of the current working directory.", I have no idea which configuration Biome loaded or what the CWD might be.


### Expected result

The message should contain all information that Biome used when it decided that this was an error because that will make it much easier for the user to do something about the problem.

In this case, please include the absolute path to the configuration file that Biome found and the absolute path to the current working directory.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9463.title: 🐛 Improve error message: Biome found a configuration file outside of the current working directory.

biomejs/biome#9464.body:
### Description

`Temporal` is a proposal that reached stage 4 yesterday and has been implemented and enabled by default in Chrome and Firefox, with platforms like node looking to enable it soon. we should add it to our globals list so that lint rules like `noUndeclaredVariables` don't trigger a false positive
biomejs/biome#9464.title: 📎 Add `Temporal` to list of globals

biomejs/biome#9477.body:
### Environment information

Setting source.organizeImports.biome to "explicit" or "never" in editor.codeActionsOnSave does not prevent import sorting on save. The only way to prevent sorting is to also set `source.fixAll.biome` to `never`

<details>


```bash
  - Biome: 2.4.5
  - VS Code Biome extension: 3.4.1
  - OS: macOS

```

</details>


### What happened?

  1. Set workspace .vscode/settings.json:
  ```
  {
    "editor.formatOnSave": true,
    "editor.codeActionsOnSave": {
      "source.organizeImports.biome": "explicit",
      "source.fixAll.biome": "explicit"
    },
    "editor.defaultFormatter": "biomejs.biome"
  }
```
  2. Open a .tsx file and manually reorder an import to be out of alphabetical order
  3. Save the file

  Expected: Imports remain in the manually reordered position

  Actual: Imports are sorted on save

  Additional context

  - Setting both to "never" does prevent sorting — only "explicit" is broken
  - Also tested with "editor.formatOnSave": false and both code actions set to "explicit" — imports still sorted
  - Adding the generic "source.organizeImports": "never" alongside the Biome-specific setting did not help

### Expected result

Imports remain in the manually reordered position

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/biome#9477.title: 🐛 source.organizeImports.biome: "explicit" still sorts imports on save

### What happened?

When running biome (locally and in CI) I get this error:

```bash
Biome encountered an unexpected error

This is a bug in Biome, not an error in your code, and we would appreciate it if you could report it to https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/ along with the following information to help us fixing the issue.

When opening the issue, please provide a minimal reproduction, or identify and share the file/code that triggers it. Without a way to reproduce the error, the error can't be fixed:

Source Location: crates/biome_js_semantic/src/semantic_model/scope.rs:150:38
Thread Name: biome::workspace_worker_2
Message: no entry found for key
Stack Trace:
   0: <unknown>
   1: <unknown>
   2: <unknown>
   3: <unknown>
   4: <unknown>
   5: <unknown>
   6: <unknown>
   7: <unknown>
   8: <unknown>
   9: <unknown>
  10: <unknown>
  11: <unknown>
  12: <unknown>
  13: <unknown>
  14: <unknown>
  15: <unknown>
  16: <unknown>
  17: <unknown>
  18: <unknown>
  19: <unknown>
  20: <unknown>
  21: <unknown>
  22: <unknown>
  23: <unknown>
  24: <unknown>

<<filepath>> internalError/panic  INTERNAL  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ processing panicked: no entry found for key
  
  ⚠ This diagnostic was derived from an internal Biome error. Potential bug, please report it if necessary.

```

I realize the `unknown` spam output may not be super helpful, and even worse I cannot reproduce this in the playground, but I've narrowed it down a bit; it seems to be complaining due to the multiple `constructor` signatures; when I remove them in favor of the final one I no longer get the panic. I can reproduce locally with code as simple as this:

```ts
export class Foo<O extends Record<string, any>> {
  name: string;
  options: O;

  constructor(name: string, opts?: 0);
  constructor(options: O)
  constructor(nameOrOptions: string | O, opts?: O) {
    this.name = typeof nameOrOptions === 'string' ? nameOrOptions : 'unknown';
    this.options = opts ?? {} as O;
  }
} 
```

I have a [simple repro repository](https://github.com/planttheidea/biome-repro-1773506130622) as the playground is insufficient.

### Expected result

No panic is created, naturally haha.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### Rule name

noUselessStringConcat

### Playground link

https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26020376/Reproduction.zip

### Expected result

`noUselessStringConcat` safe fix should not transform strings concatenated with template functions.

Current rule fix transforms this
```js
t`translate-me` + "!"
```
into this
```js
"translate-me!"
```
which is wrong.

Reproduction:
1) download the [Reproduction.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26020376/Reproduction.zip)
2) cd ./Reproduction/
3) `npx -p @biomejs/biome@2.4.7 biome lint --only lint/complexity/noUselessStringConcat ./demo.*`
4) check the terminal output and see how Biome suggests (and with --write transforms) a wrong fix.

### Rule name

lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables

### Playground link

https://biomejs.dev/playground/?

### Expected result

After upgrading from 2.4.6 → 2.4.7 I'm seeing new Biome failures where TypeScript type usage is no longer being considered as variable usage, eg:

```ts
export default function MyComponent() {
  // In 2.4.6 Biome would correctly detect this as variable usage.
  // In 2.4.7 Biome now falsely reports this as a `lint/correctness/noUnusedVariables` failure
  const { a } = useThing();

  return someFunction<typeof a>();
}
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

### What happened?

There seems to be a bug with the `noNegationElse` fixer, it's not handling comments properly in ternaries (2.4.9):
```diff
   const keyName =
-    obj[val] !== undefined
+    obj[val] === undefined
       ? // TS reverse mapped enum
-        (obj[val] as string)
-      : // Normal enum / `const object`
         // TODO: Figure out a way to cache the names of commonly-used enum numbers for performance if needed
-        (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string);
+        (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string)
+      : (obj[val] as string);
```
```ts
// before
  const keyName =
    obj[val] !== undefined
      ? // TS reverse mapped enum <-- not moved, becomes wrongly positioned after fixer is run
        (obj[val] as string)
      : // Normal enum / `const object` <-- deleted entirely
        // TODO: Figure out a way to cache the names of commonly-used enum numbers for performance if needed
        (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string);
// after
  const keyName =
    obj[val] === undefined
      ? // TS reverse mapped enum <-- now in the wrong spot
        // TODO: Figure out a way to cache the names of commonly-used enum numbers for performance if needed
        (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string)
      : (obj[val] as string);
```

Minimal reproduction: https://github.com/DayKev/no-negation-else
(The playground doesn't seem to be applying the fixer for `noNegationElse` for some reason ([example](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?lineWidth=120&indentStyle=space&lintRules=noNegationElse&analyzerFixMode=safeAndUnsafeFixes&tab=formatter&pane=Diagnostics&%2BACwAIAB2AGEAbAApACAAYQBzACAAcwB0AHIAaQBuAGcAKQA7AA%3D%3D&language=ts)) so I made a repo instead)

### Expected result

I would expect the diff to look something like this:
```diff
   const keyName =
-    obj[val] !== undefined
-      ? // TS reverse mapped enum
-        (obj[val] as string)
-      : // Normal enum / `const object`
+    obj[val] === undefined
+      ? // Normal enum / `const object`
         // TODO: Figure out a way to cache the names of commonly-used enum numbers for performance if needed
-        (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string);
+        (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string)
+      : // TS reverse mapped enum
+        (obj[val] as string);
```
Or in other words:
```ts
// input
const keyName =
  obj[val] !== undefined
    ? // TS reverse mapped enum
      (obj[val] as string)
    : // Normal enum / `const object`
      // TODO: Figure out a way to cache the names of commonly-used enum numbers for performance if needed
      (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string);

// expected output
const keyName =
  obj[val] === undefined
    ? // Normal enum / `const object`
      // TODO: Figure out a way to cache the names of commonly-used enum numbers for performance if needed
      (testEnumValueToKey(obj as NormalEnum<E>, val) as string)
    : // TS reverse mapped enum
      (obj[val] as string);
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct

biomejs/biome#9640.body:
### Environment information

main

### What happened?

The current markdown

```md
[link](   /uri
  "title"  )
```

Emits the following CST

```
0: MD_DOCUMENT@0..28
  0: (empty)
  1: MD_BLOCK_LIST@0..28
    0: MD_PARAGRAPH@0..28
      0: MD_INLINE_ITEM_LIST@0..28
        0: MD_INLINE_LINK@0..27
          0: L_BRACK@0..1 "[" [] []
          1: MD_INLINE_ITEM_LIST@1..5
            0: MD_TEXTUAL@1..5
              0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@1..5 "link" [] []
          2: R_BRACK@5..6 "]" [] []
          3: L_PAREN@6..7 "(" [] []
          4: MD_INLINE_ITEM_LIST@7..17
            0: MD_TEXTUAL@7..10
              0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@7..10 "   " [] []
            1: MD_TEXTUAL@10..14
              0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@10..14 "/uri" [] []
            2: MD_TEXTUAL@14..15
              0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@14..15 "\n" [] []
            3: MD_TEXTUAL@15..16
              0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@15..16 " " [] []
            4: MD_TEXTUAL@16..17
              0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@16..17 " " [] []
          5: MD_LINK_TITLE@17..24
            0: MD_INLINE_ITEM_LIST@17..24
              0: MD_TEXTUAL@17..24
                0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@17..24 "\"title\"" [] []
          6: R_PAREN@24..27 "  )" [] []
        1: MD_TEXTUAL@27..28
          0: MD_TEXTUAL_LITERAL@27..28 "\n" [] []
      1: (empty)
  2: EOF@28..28 "" [] []
```

The token `R_PAREN@24..27 "  )" [] []` is incorrect. The token contains a space when it shouldn't. cc @jfmcdowell 

### Expected result

The space should belong to the previous nodes or marked as a trivia, and the token should be free of spaces.

### What happened?

There is a parse error started to occur in Astro files for certain Regular Expressions in the frontmatter, e.g.:

```
---
const RE = /\d{4}/
---
```

<details>
<summary>The error message</summary>

```
/main.astro:4:1 parse ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ expected `---` but instead the file ends
  
    2 │ const RE = /\d{4}/
    3 │ ---
  > 4 │ 
      │ 
  
  ℹ the file ends here
  
    2 │ const RE = /\d{4}/
    3 │ ---
  > 4 │ 
      │ 
  
/main.astro:3:3 parse ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  ✖ Invalid assignment to `-`
  
    1 │ ---
    2 │ const RE = /\d{4}/
  > 3 │ ---
      │   ^
    4 │ 
  
  ℹ This expression cannot be assigned to

```

</details>

### Reproduction

[**Playground Link**](https://biomejs.dev/playground/?tab=formatter&pane=Diagnostics&code=LQAtAC0ACgBjAG8AbgBzAHQAIABSAEUAIAA9ACAALwBcAGQAewA0AH0ALwAKAC0ALQAtAAoA&language=astro)

### Expected result

It should be parsed correctly

### Expected result

The second `PLUS` token shouldn't have skipped tokens, instead the spaces should be parsed and belong to `MD_INDENT_TOKEN_LIST`

Mixed ordered-list delimiters are merged across blank lines.

biomejs/biome#9771.body:
## Summary

When a Markdown header appears inside a bullet list item (e.g. CommonMark spec example 259), the parser produces an `MdHeader` node with a non-standard slot layout — the first syntax slot (`MdIndentTokenList`) is missing or empty.

This causes a panic/error in the formatter when it tries to destructure `MdHeaderFields` normally.

## Reproduction

Run the formatter against a Markdown document containing a header inside a bullet list item, such as:

```markdown
- # Foo
- Bar
  ---
  baz
```

Without a workaround, the test `markdown::spec::example_259_md` in `crates/biome_markdown_formatter` will fail.

## Current workaround

A guard was added in the formatter (PR #9693, commit on branch `md-formatter-basic`) to detect the missing slot and fall back to verbatim formatting:

```rust
// Headers inside list items can have a non-standard slot layout
// (e.g. missing MdIndentTokenList in slot 0). Fall back to verbatim.
if node.syntax().slots().next().is_none_or(|s| s.into_node().is_none()) {
    return format_verbatim_node(node.syntax()).fmt(f);
}
```

This workaround should be removed once the parser correctly handles this case.

## Expected behaviour

The parser should always produce a well-formed `MdHeader` node with the expected slot layout, regardless of whether the header appears at the top level or inside a list item.

## References

- PR introducing the workaround: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/pull/9693
- Formatter review comment: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/pull/9693#discussion_r3022711581
- Raised by: @tidefield (workaround), flagged as parser bug by @ematipico
biomejs/biome#9771.title: fix(markdown_parser): non-standard slot layout for MdHeader inside list items

biomejs/biome-vscode#817.body:
### VS Code version

1.104

### Extension version

3.2.0

### Biome version

2.2.6

### Operating system

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Description

Hello,

In vscode when htting cmd+shift+p and selecting "Format document with..." multiple biome entries show up in the list, in my case always 2 entries. 

This only happens in a VS Code workspace with multiple folders, where one of the folders is pointing to the root of the project. Here is a comment from myself which explains how to reproduce this issue:

https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/1584#issuecomment-3389149860

This seems to cause random issues where formatting files randomly break the file by adding duplicate bad code at the end of the file or messing up imports. I tested a full week of work without having my Root folder, so by removing the multiple biome instance issue, and could not encounter any of those random bugs when formatting a file. I assume the multiple biome instances are causing this.

This makes using the vscode biome extension a big hassle, if I remove those random issues by removing the Root folder then I won't have access to a good UX in my monorepo (git disabled, root file exploration missing etc). Working with the bug active is maybe even worse because of those random file corruption which seems to happen every 5-10 minutes and makes you lose time correcting those files.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Setup a workspace with multiple folders where one of the folders is pointing to the root of the project
2. Add biome at the root of the project only. No other folder has biome installed
3. Hit cmd+shift+P, click on "Format document with...", see multiple entries of biome appear
4. You can remove the multiple entries bug by simple removing the Root folder from the workspace settings
5. The double biome entries seem to conflict with each other and causes random file corruption issues when formatting.

### Expected behavior

No multiple biome entries show up when having a Root folder in my workspace settings and hitting "Format document with...". 
No formatting bugs which corrupts files which seems to be linked to those multiple biome instances conflicting.

### Does this issue occur when using the CLI directly?

No

### Link to a minimal reproduction

https://github.com/Jylth/biome_multiple_entries_vscode_workspace_repro.git

1. Open the workspace file using vscode
2. pnpm install
3. Hit cmd+shift+P => "Format document with...", see multiple biome entries

### Logs

No errors in the logs
biomejs/biome-vscode#817.title: 🐛 Multiple biome entries from vscode workspace causes corrupted formatting

biomejs/website#2305.body:
### Documentation URL

https://next.biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-unwanted-polyfillio/

### Description

Ref: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/pull/4731

Polyfill.io was sold to a unknown company who used it to distribute malicious code via a supply chain attack. Since it was detected in Jun 2024 it's been offline after being blocked by major DNS providers. 

Sources:
- https://blog.qualys.com/vulnerabilities-threat-research/2024/06/28/polyfill-io-supply-chain-attack
- https://censys.com/blog/july-2-polyfill-io-supply-chain-attack-digging-into-the-web-of-compromised-domains
- https://fossa.com/blog/polyfill-supply-chain-attack-details-fixes/

As a result the docs should only reference the replacement domains in order to:
1. provide working examples (as polyfill.io is still blocked/offline)
2. protect biome users against them using it in the event the domain returns

The Fastly domain is already referenced, I'd suggest including the Cloudflare one too and removing references to the original. Cloudflare is already supported in the code, so no changes are required there.

`https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/polyfill/v3/polyfill.min.js?version=4.8.0`

Making this an issue now so it isn't lost - if no one gets a chance to update it then I'll have a crack at it soon once my time opens up a little.

### Expectations

Remove references to the original polyfill.io

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow Biome's Code of Conduct
biomejs/website#2305.title: 📚 📎 noUnwantedPolyfillio should be updated to only showcase replacement domains

tamasfe/taplo#715.body:
**tl;dr**: I can't see myself returning here, and I'm asking you to decide the fate of this project.

When I started working on taplo, I wanted it to be a tool that puts TOML on par with YAML and JSON in terms of dev experience and perhaps even surpass them, in some aspects I think I achieved that and I'm happy that it ended up being useful for many. However the project was pretty much a learning experience for me, it was the first parser I have written, the first LSP, etc. and it really shows.

I still have a lot of plans, for example:

- rework the parser so that it is not a mess (e.g. I really liked the approach I ended up with in [rhai LSP](https://github.com/rhaiscript/lsp/blob/master/crates/rhai-rowan/src/parser/parsers/mod.rs), I think `rust-analyzer` has a similar architecture)
- adjust the code that builds on top of it so that it won't rely on an [accidentally introduced behavior that actually makes no sense](https://github.com/tamasfe/taplo/blob/a8bc571ee28775e7d5ad84c2ea87cf5b61ab42f5/crates/taplo/src/parser/mod.rs#L185)
- rethink/rework whatever the hell is happening after syntax parsing, to date I still have no idea how to efficiently and cleanly turn TOML into a DOM with its arbitrary rules and parent nodes appearing out of order
- rebase the LSP onto a maintained library, like [tower-lsp](https://github.com/ebkalderon/tower-lsp)
- rework the formatter so that it handles line breaks and nesting properly, something like [prettyplease](https://github.com/dtolnay/prettyplease) or [rhai-fmt](https://github.com/rhaiscript/lsp/tree/master/crates/rhai-fmt) (that was also based on prettyplease).
- fix filepath/URL handling, I think there is still confusion around this (unless the `windows<->everything else` conflicts were fixed while I was away)
- schemas and in general position querying (which TOML node maps to which property in schemas) is quite buggy
- ... probably many more (looking at the open issues)

I have practically disappeared from this project for the past 1-2 years, and now it is safe to say that whatever my plans are, they are very unlikely to be fulfilled. Nowadays I have a lot less free time, and even in that free time I code less as a hobby. On top of that being a maintainer really feels like a 2nd or 3rd job and I have a lot of respect for people who can pull it off, I definitely cannot.

On top of that, I don't feel like that the amount of work I listed above (probably a month worth?) is worth putting into this tool, while it is useful, I don't feel anyone would really miss it if it disappeared overnight.

So now I'm officially stepping down as a maintainer/owner/whatever, I practically haven't been one for years anyway.

Thank you @panekj @ia0 @JounQin and everyone else who helped keep this alive.

My question is **what should we do with all of this?**
- transfer everything to an organization?
- keep the current state up with sporadic maintenance until the TOML spec changes and everything breaks?
- just abandon/archive everything?
- ultimately is there anyone who would like to pour time into this and take over?

The following needs to be thought of:
 - [ ] this repo
 - [ ] access to the extension on [openvsx](https://open-vsx.org/extension/tamasfe/even-better-toml)
 - [ ] access to the extension on the [vscode marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=tamasfe.even-better-toml)
 - [ ] access to the `@taplo` organization on NPM
 - [ ] access the [`taplo`](https://pypi.org/project/taplo/) package on pypi (I don't have anything to do with this)
 - [ ] access to the libraries on crates.io

I'm open to anything really and suggestions are welcome.
tamasfe/taplo#715.title: The future of the project

zed-industries/zed#39803.body:
### Summary

Despite setting `require_config_file: true` in the global Zed settings (~/.config/zed/settings.json), the Biome LSP still activates and runs when opening projects that do not have a `biome.json` configuration file. This behavior contradicts the expected functionality of the `require_config_file` setting, which should prevent Biome from running in projects without its configuration file.

This issue appears to be a regression or incomplete fix of the problem that was supposedly addressed in PR #35306. While that PR fixed the formatter behavior, the LSP itself still activates in projects without biome.json.

**Global Settings (~/.config/zed/settings.json):**

<details><summary>Relevant configuration</summary>

```json
{
  "lsp": {
    "biome": {
      "settings": {
        "require_config_file": true
      }
    }
  }
}
```

</details>

### Steps to reproduce:

1. Set `require_config_file: true` for Biome in your global Zed settings (~/.config/zed/settings.json)
2. Create or open a project that uses ESLint (or any other linter) but does **NOT** have a `biome.json` file in its root
3. Open any JavaScript/TypeScript file in that project
4. Observe Biome LSP diagnostics appearing in the editor

**Expected Behavior**: 
Biome should NOT activate or provide any diagnostics/formatting in projects that lack a `biome.json` configuration file when `require_config_file: true` is set.

**Actual Behavior**: 
Biome LSP activates regardless of the `require_config_file` setting and starts linting/analyzing files even though no biome.json exists in the project.

### Additional Context

This issue has been reported multiple times in the Biome extension repository:
- biomejs/biome-zed#59 (Open since Oct 2024, 17+ reactions)
- biomejs/biome-zed#126 (Closed but issue persists)
- biomejs/biome#6589 (Confirmed bug)

While PR #35306 was supposed to fix this issue, it appears to have only partially resolved the formatter behavior but not the LSP activation itself.

**Possible Impact:**
- Users working on multiple projects with different linter configurations experience conflicts
- Biome diagnostics appear in projects that don't use Biome, causing confusion
- Cannot enforce project-specific tooling choices through configuration

**Related Issues:**
- #35306 (Previous fix - incomplete)
- biomejs/biome-zed#59
- biomejs/biome-zed#126

### Zed Version and System Specs

Zed: v0.206.7 (Zed) 
OS: Linux Wayland ubuntu 24.04
Memory: 13.5 GiB
Architecture: x86_64
GPU: AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV RENOIR) || radv || Mesa 25.0.7-0ubuntu0.24.04.2
zed-industries/zed#39803.title: Biome LSP still activates in projects without biome.json despite `require_config_file: true`
